Workaround: To workaround this issue it is necessary to perform the following steps:Change to the directory fullfile(matlabroot, 'toolbox\rtw\targets\mpc555dk\mpc555dk')Edit the file make_codewarrior_tfl.m and search for the string 'roundf'. Delete the line which contains the string 'roundf' and save the fileDelete the file make_codewarrior_tfl.pRun the command make_mpc555_targetfcnlib_mat_filesCheck date modified on the following files to see that they have been updated: codewarrior_tfl_tmw.mat and diab_tfl_tmw.mat

Summary: Invalid error message for periodic function-call subsystem whose function call initiator is itself inside a triggered subsystem
Description: Setting the Sample time type  of a function-call subsystem's trigger port block to periodic and specifying a sample time means that the subsystem must be  called at the specified rate. If the function-call initiator for such a subsystem is inside a  different function-call subsystem, Simulink displays an error, instead of allowing the runtime check to determine whether that periodic subsystem is called at the specified rate.  For example, if the function-call initiator is inside another function-call subsystem that executes every time step at the specified rate and the function-call initiator executes at every time step, the periodic subsystem should execute once per time step and there should be no error reported.

Summary: Simulink reports errors when updating a diagram containing Data Store Memory blocks
Description: Suppose the data type or signal type property of a Data Store Memory block in your model is set to a value other than auto . If you start the simulation, pause it, and then update the diagram, Simulink displays errors similar to the following: Cannot change port data type of 'modelname/Data Store Read' while simulation is running.Cannot change port data type of 'modelname/Data Store Write' while simulation is running.

Summary: For certain modes of the Mathematical Amplifier block, the AM/PM characteristic wrongly depends on IIP3
Description: The Mathematical Amplifier block can use two mathematical models of nonlinearities -- a cubic polynomial and a hyperbolic tangent. When these modes are chosen, the signal is scaled according to a function of the input third intercept point (IIP3). The AM/PM distortions are then applied to the scaled version of the signal. However, the AM/PM characteristic should not depend on the value of IIP3.

Summary: Subsystems: right-click builds fail when a block in a subsystem uses a custom model parameter
Description: If you do the following, Real-Time Workshop fails to build an executable for a subsystem:  You add a custom model parameter to a block in the subsystem. For example, add_param(model , 'ParameterName', value )   You use the right-click build mechanism to build the subsystem.   When the build fails, the following error message appears: Error using ==> set_paramblock_diagram does not have a parameter named 'ParameterName'.

Summary: Adding thermal noise and phase noise together does not give a symmetric spectrum.
Description: When both thermal noise and phase noise are simulated in an RF mixer, the total noise spectrum is not symmetric with respect to the center frequency. However, it should be symmetric.

Summary: Viewer displays objects incorrectly when scene is built using V-Realm Builder.
Description: Building a scene using V-Realm Builder by dragging various objects from the object library to the scene causes incorrect naming of object vertex coordinates, resulting in incorrect references to them. This causes incorrect rendering within the Virtual Reality Toolbox viewer. V-Realm names all the object "Coord" fields with the same DEF name suffixed with "_v2%0".  Subsequently, inside each object, it calls "Coord USE _v2%0" to reference this field to define various object sub-shapes.  Two VRML errors are produced as a result:   In the file, there is more than one object with the DEF name "_v2%0"The "USE _v2%0" refers to the last "DEF _v2%0" object coordinates for all object's shapes, creating a visual mismatch.
Workaround: To work around this issue, after you create scenes with V-Realm:Edit the .WRL file in a text editor (or, preferably VRMLPad, a context-sensitive, syntax-checking, text based VRML editor). For individual objects, look for the "coord DEF _v2%0" strings. Replace each "_v2%0" with something more descriptive.  E.g. replace "USE _v2%0" with, say, "USE CAR1_Coord". Then go to the next object, say, SOFA, and do the same.  Rename the coord and the USE references to it. Sometimes there are more "DEF coord _v2%0 ..." fields inside one object, instead of one DEF and several USEs.  You can delete all the identical coord fields except the first one, and make USE references instead.

Summary: Conditional subsystems: code generated for Outport of conditional subsystem does not compile
Description: If a conditionally executed subsystem includes an Outport block that specifies an initial condition other than [] and receives a wide signal, Real-Time Workshop might not be able to compile the code it generates for the subsystem.
Workaround: Set the Loop unrolling threshold option on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box to a value higher than the width of the signal entering the Outport block.

Summary: TLC error while generating code for model that does not include both DM642 EVM Video ADC and Video DAC blocks
Description: When a Simulink model contains only a DM642 EVM Video ADC or only a DM642 EVM Video DAC block, dm642evm_video_shared.tlc  returns an error about a non-existent variable when you try to generate code from the model.
Workaround: Include both DM642 EVM Video ADC and the DM642 EVM Video DAC blocks in your model, even if you are not using one of them. Ground or terminate the input or output lines of the block that is not being used.

Summary: dicominfo returns incomplete results for files with sequences in private attributes
Description: If dicomread  encounters a file with sequences of attributes inside a private attribute ˇŞ an attribute with an odd group number ˇŞ it stops reading metadata prematurely.

Summary: Multitask data store diagnostic does not work for global data stores
Description: The Multitask data store  diagnostic applies to global as well as local data stores. However, when a multitask data integrity error involving a global data store occurs, Simulink ignores this diagnostic's setting (none , warning , or error ) and always reports an error.

Summary: Preempted entity arriving at another server can cause exception or incorrect behavior
Description: If the P  entity output port of a Single Server block connects to the entity input port of another server block, then during the simulation you might see an exception or incorrect behavior. Incorrect behaviors can includeAn error dialog that mentions scheduling events in the past Erroneous blockage at the P  output port
Workaround: Insert a FIFO Queue block with infinite capacity between the two servers.

Summary: Digital Filter Design block generates errors and freezes FDATool if Inverse Sinc Lowpass is selected
Description: FDATool generates a hard error when you select Inverse Sinc Lowpass  under Filter Type . This error occurs only after you have stored a filter previously or loaded a saved filter or session. This error also occurs if you launch FDATool from the Signal Processing Blockset Digital Filter Design block.
Workaround: To fix the bug in releases prior to R2006a, download and unzip the attached file. Then place the p-code file setcomponentstate.p in the following directory:?MATLABROOT?\toolbox\signal\sigtools\@siggui\@designpanel

Summary: Covariance modelling produces incorrect models
Description: If you select a covariance model when setting up a local model in the Model Browser, the resulting local model will be not be fitted correctly.
Workaround: In order to produce correct models in version 3.0, covariance modelling must be turned off. You can do this by selecting the local model node in the Model Browser and then choosing the Model...Set Up menu item.  This will open the Local Model Setup dialog.  Change the Covariance Model selection to "None".

Summary: Embedded MATLAB Function block generates uncompilable code containing &&
Description: The Embedded MATLAB Function block produces uncompilable code in cases that often involve scalar indexing of two-dimensional input signals.  However, through function inlining, there may also be implicit uses of scalar indexing that cause the problem. One way to diagnose the bug is to look for improper use of the &&  operator in the generated code.This bug also affects the Truth Table Block, and Stateflow Embedded MATLAB Functions and Truth Tables.
Workaround: You may be able to work around this issue by perturbing your code to avoid scalar indexing.  For example, use temporary variables to access two-dimensional input data, as in this example for an input u:  tu = u(:);Use tu instead of u to access the input data.

Summary: FVTOOL does not save the sampling frequency correctly when you use Save as Default
Description: When you set a new default sampling frequency in FVTool by clicking Save as Default , FVTool does not save the new frequency as the default. If you relaunch FVTool, you see the original sampling frequency, not your new one.

Summary: linmod ignores initial states specified in the Configuration Parameters dialog.
Description: linmod and dlinmod will ignore any initial state values specified in the Data Import/Export panel of the Configuration Parameters dialog, when you use the following syntax:[A,B,C,D] = linmod(mdl)or[A,B,C,D] = dlinmod(mdl,ts)where mdl is the model name and ts is the desired sample time.
Workaround: You can use the following syntax to provide the initial state values to linmod and dlinmod:[A,B,C,D] = linmod(mdl,x0)or[A,B,C,D] = dlinmod(mdl,ts,x0)where x0 is a vector or structure of state values. See the help for linmod for more information on creating vectors and structures of state values.A second workaround is to download an updated version of dlinmod:Go to your directory [matlabroot]\toolbox\simulink\simulinkRename the file dlinmod.m to dlinmod.bakDownload the attached zip file which contains dlinmod.m and save it within the [matlabroot]\toolbox\simulink\simulink directory

Summary: Model coverage can report incorrect decision coverage for states with crossing transitions
Description: The Model Coverage Tool can report incorrect decision coverage for a Stateflow diagram that includes both of the following: A superstate containing mutually exclusive states A transition that originates on a substate and crosses the superstate boundary  In this situation, the Model Coverage Tool associates a coverage point related to exiting the superstate with the crossing transition, resulting in incorrect decision coverage for the diagram.

Summary: Why can't I deploy an application that uses the Matrox adaptor?
Description: If I create an application using the Image Acquisition Toolbox's "matrox" adaptor, it works fine when run in MATLAB or when the compiled application is run on the development machine, but I receive errors when I try to deploy and run the application on other machines.
Workaround: To work around this bug, follow these steps:Backup or rename the file <matlabroot>\toolbox\imaq\imaqadaptors\win32\mwmatroximaq.dll.Download the attached zip file.Extract the attached zip file to \toolbox.This should add two files to your MATLAB installation:A new version of <matlabroot>\toolbox\imaq\imaqadaptors\win32\mwmatroximaq.dll<matlabroot>\toolbox\imaq\imaq\private\privateGetMatlabRoot.mRestart MATLAB.Recompile your application.

Summary: Incorrect numbering of the taps of the Multi-Winding Transformer block
Description: When you try delete taps from a Multi-Winding Transformer block, the remaining taps are numbered incorrectly.

Summary: Phase noise level varies with finite impulse response filter length.
Description: When the ratio of the sampling frequency to the finite impulse response filter length is greater than the smallest phase noise frequency offset, changing the finite impulse response filter length of the Input Port block causes the phase noise level from the simulation to vary. However, the phase noise level should not depend on the finite impulse response filter length.
Workaround: The workaround is to specify the finite impulse response filter length, N, such that Fs/N is smaller than half of the smallest phase noise frequency offset, where Fs is the sampling frequency.

Summary: Problems using Multimeter block with Accelerator mode
Description: When the Accelerator mode is in service, you get an error message in the command window if you try to open the Multimeter block mask: Accelerator mode is not supported through the S-Function API.

Summary: S-function can fail to inherit sample times
Description: When propagating sample times, Simulink may fail to assign sample times to an S-function that specifies the PORT_SAMPLE_TIMES_ASSIGNED  option because this option indicates that the S-function sets all of its sample times itself. This can lead to unexpected simulation results and/or warnings such as the following: Warning: Inconsistent sample times. Sample time of signal ([-1, -1]) driving input port 4 of 'mCommonInit_4/ss/b2/b1' differs from the expected sample time at this input port ([-1, 0]).  Warning: Sample time for 'mCommonInit_4/ss/b2/b1' output 2 was not set.
Workaround: Ensure that any S-function that specifies PORT_SAMPLE_TIMES_ASSIGNED sets all of its block and port sample times in its mdlInitializeSize or mdlImitializeSampleTimes methods.

Summary: Error when Automated Tradeoff command is used on a multimodel tradeoff
Description: If you try to use the Automated Tradeoff menu item from a tradeoff that contains multimodels (models that switch between distinct sub-models for different table cells) the following error appears in the MATLAB command window after an optimization has been selected:??? Index exceeds matrix dimensions.??? Error while evaluating uimenu Callback. The automated tradeoff process does not then proceed to the next step.

Summary: Model Reference with variable-step solvers
Description: Models that use variable-step Simulink solvers may not refer, via Model blocks, to models that use SimDriveline.

Summary: Assertion during code generation for Stateflow charts containing local data as loop counter
Description: Stateflow models containing control flow loops using junctions may sometimes trigger an assertion in one of the code generation optimizations. Below is an example of what the top of the stack trace looks like when the error is triggered. Stack Trace:  [0] sf.mexw32:0x1194b8a8(0x7a735678 "genVector != 0", 0x7a735688 ".\dom_core\cg_liveness_analysis...", 218, 0)  [1] sf.mexw32:0x11870716(0, 0x7a735688 ".\dom_core\cg_liveness_analysis...", 218, 0x7a735678 "genVector != 0")  [2] cg_ir.dll:void __cdecl client_assertion_failed(char const *,int,char const *)(0x7a735688 ".\dom_core\cg_liveness_analysis...", 218, 0x7a735678 "genVector != 0", 0x13d272e0) + 27 bytes  [3] cg_ir.dll:public: bool __thiscall CG::Liveness::varIsLiveOnEdge(class CG_Edge *,class CG_Var *)(0x13975388, 0x13d36f60, 0x13b64d18, 0x00ccd178) + 49 bytes  [4] cg_ir.dll:public: struct PtrSet * __thiscall CG::LoopFusion::computeLoopsWithLiveIndexVarOnExit(void)(0, 0x13b64d18, 0x00ccd1a8 "???", 0x7a6d071c) + 124 bytes  [5] cg_ir.dll:public: __thiscall CG::LoopFusion::LoopFusion(class CG_Const *,bool)(0x13b64d18, 1, 0x13b64d18, 0x7a73598c) + 98 bytes  [6] cg_ir.dll:_cg_loop_fusion_2(0x13b64d18, 1, 0x13d36dc0, 0x031f2078) + 44 bytes
Workaround: Type the following command in MATLAB to turn off the optimization prior to using Stateflow:sf('feature', 'For-loop raising','off');

Summary: Calibration constants are not loaded correctly from old project files
Description: In rare circumstances, the CAGE tool may fail to correctly load projects from previous versions of the software.  The bug will cause the Calibration Manager to fail to open, and the affected feature will not evaluate in the Surface Viewer.
Workaround: A patch is available that will allow the affected project files to load correctly.  To install this fix:Rename the file $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcdata\@cgexprmodel\loadobj.m to $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcdata\@cgexprmodel\loadobj.m.old. Download the file loadobj.m that is listed in the Attachments section and save it to $MATLAB\toolbox\mbc\mbcdata\@cgexprmodel\loadobj.m. Restart MATLAB.

Summary: LSF scheduler does not work with DataLocation that contains any blank spaces
Description: If the DataLocation  supplied to the LSF scheduler contains a space, the submission string to LSF is not correctly quoted, and it fails.

Summary: MCRInstaller.exe fails when run from encrypted folder on Win XP
Description: If the MCRInstaller is run from an encrypted folder on Win XP, the installation fails with the following error: "The system cannot open the device or file specified." After canceling out of the installation, another message is displayed: "The installer has encountered an unexpected error installing this package. This may indicate a problem with this package. The error code is 2755."
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Code generation takes a long time for Stateflow charts with large vector/matrix local data initialized using a Parameter
Description: Stateflow charts with vector/marix local data initialized from workspace  parameter of the same name generate unrolled loops for their initialization as below:Dwork.localData[0] = rtP.localDataParam[0];Dwork.localData[1] = rtP.localDataParam[1];Dwork.localData[2] = rtP.localDataParam[2]; As a result, the chart-initializer function becomes excessively large for vectors/matrices of large sizes (in this example, it is a [300x46] matrix). This triggers an inefficiency in one of our optimizations, which exhibits quadratic behavior for extremely long, straight-line code that does not use control statements (such as if   and switch  ). The inefficiency results in long code generation times.
Workaround: To work around this issue, follow these steps:Change the name of the local data object so it does not collide with the parameter name.Create a new data object with the same name as the parameter and set its scope to Parameter.Change the initialization method for the local data object from Parameter to Expression and set the initial value to a constant, such as zero.In the default transition paths for the chart, use an explicit assignment statement to set the value of the local data object to the value of the parameter data object.For example, if the local data object is named Klocal and the parameter data object is named K, use this assignment statement:Klocal = K;

Summary: dicomread and dicominfo fail for files with group 0002 data but no preamble
Description: According to the specification, DICOM files should all begin with a 132-byte preamble, followed by file and message metadata, and the imagery.  dicomread  and dicominfo  can actually handle many files that are missing the preamble and file-related metadata. However, for files that contain the file-related metadata (group 0002)but that are missing the preamble, dicominfo  gives the following error: ??? Error using ==> medformats\private\dicom_set_mmeta_encodingCould not determine file encoding. dicomread  gives this message and returns empty: Group = 0002 (2)Warning: Suspicious fragmentary file does not start with group 0008.It might not be DICOM.

Summary: Fixed-step sample time error message does not list all sample times that exist in model
Description: The following error message for fixed-step solvers is incorrect: The sample time properties specified on the Solver page are not consistent with the sample times in the model. The periodic sample time constraints specified are: [Sample time, Offset time] [a, b] And the periodic sample times in the model are: [Sample time, Offset time] [0, 0] [a2, b2] [a3, b3] The error message should not include [0, 0]  because itis not a valid periodic sample time. The error message alsofails to include the model's slowest periodic sample time.

Summary: State inconsistency check disabled when exiting a substate
Description: If a substate is exited during simulation, the state inconsistency check is turned off. As a result, you will not be notified of inconsistencies at runtime. The simulation will contine, but won't progress beyond the state that contains the inconsistency.A state inconsistency occurs when no default transition can be taken to a substate.
Workaround: You will be warned at compile time of potential state inconsistency errors. Check the offending superstate for a missing unconditional default transition.

Summary: Incorrect "on event_name" action coverage results for Stateflow charts
Description: The model coverage tool reports incorrect coverage results for on event_name  actions associated with superstates that contain exclusive (OR) substates. This problem results from an internal indexing error.
Workaround: There are currently no known workarounds.

Summary: Model reference does not work with global tunable parameters without an Accelerator or RTW license
Description: Model reference does not support global tunable parameters when the top model is accelerated without an RTW license.

Summary: Invalid sample time error for global data store that has inherited sample time
Description: Simulink could report an invalid sample time error associated with the usage of a global data store that has an inherited sample time.

Summary: Issues with Draw Shapes blocks and code generation with Real-Time Workshop
Description: If you have a Draw Shapes block whose Shapes parameter is set to Rectangle or Circle followed by another Draw Shapes block with the same parameter values and the Fill shapes check box selected, the blocks produce errors during code generation with Real-Time Workshop.
Workaround: To work around this issue, you need to replace the existing svipdrawshapes.tlc file with a new version using the following steps:1) At the MATLAB command prompt, type "ver".2) Write down the version number that appears next to Video and Image Processing Blockset.3) Go to $matlabroot\toolbox\vipblks\vipmex\tlc_c. The svipdrawshapes.tlc file is located in this directory.4) Save the existing svipdrawshapes.tlc file as svipdrawshapes_old.tlc. 5) Unzip attachment_285223_20051101.zip into a temporary directory. If you have Video and Image Processing Blockset Version 1.1, copy the extracted svipdrawshapes_11.tlc file into the directory and rename it svipdrawshapes.tlc.If you have Video and Image Processing Blockset Version 1.2 or 2.0, copy the extracted svipdrawshapes_12.tlc file into the directory and rename it svipdrawshapes.tlc.

Summary: Cannot print or export TIFF files on Solaris systems
Description: When printing or exporting in the TIFF format on Solaris, the following error may be encountered in the MATLAB command window: print -dtiff testtifError using ==> printError using ==> printInvalid MEX-file '/usr/local/matlab7SP3/toolbox/matlab/imagesci/private/wtifc.mexsol':   ld.so.1: /usr/local/matlab7SP3/bin/sol2/MATLAB: fatal:   libstdc++.so.5: open failed: No such file or directory. ld.so.1:/product/matlab_v14_SP3/bin/sol2/MATLAB:fatal:   libstdc++.so.5: open failed: no such file or directory. This error occurs on Solaris systems because part of the TIFF library was built with the g++ compiler, which in turn requires certain GNU system libraries (libstdc++.so.5  and libgcc_s.so.1 ). If you encounter this error, the operating system on your computer does not have these libraries. This is only a problem on Solaris systems.
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can either obtain the necessary GNU system libraries (libstdc++.so.5 and libgcc_s.so.1), or you can replace the TIFF library in your existing MATLAB installation with the attached TIFF library that was built using Sun's compiler.To replace the TIFF library, follow these steps:Rename the TIFF library in your existing MATLAB installation. cd $MATLABROOT/bin/sol2mv libtiff.so.3.7.1 libtiff.so.3.7.1.OLDDownload the attached file and unzip libtiff.so.3.7.1, to the same location.

Summary: Linearizing models with model references can cause memory segmentation violations
Description: Attempting to linearizing a model that references other models can can cause segmentation violations under the following circumstances:You are using any of the following tools to linearize the model: trim  linmod  or dlinmod  with the v5  option findop  linearize  using the numericalpert  option   The model contains two or more levels of model reference hierarchy.  At least one of the models in the model reference hierarchy has continuous states.  The referenced models in the second or higher level of the  model reference hierarchy do not have continuous states.
Workaround: There is no workaround for this bug.

Summary: MATLAB unexpectedly debugs mat2str.m during stop if NaN or Inf
Description: When you instruct MATLAB to stop if NaN or Inf, either by running dbstop if naninf  or by setting it via Debug > Stop if Errors/Warnings , MATLAB might enter debug mode at the top of mat2str.m , even though none of your files calls mat2str.m .
Workaround: This problem occurs because the Workspace browser's Value display calls mat2str. Use any of these workarounds:Close the Workspace browser.Hide the Workspace browser's Value column. To do so, in the Workspace browser, select View > Choose Columns and clear the Value item.Do not use dbstop if naninf mode.Ignore the breakpoint and continue running.

Summary: getcurve shows only the most recently entered leg of the curve
Description: When you input data for getcurve  by clicking in the gridded window, only the most recently entered pair of points is shown. This makes it hard to input points for an intended pattern or design.

Summary: Use of the software trigger type with National Instruments devices is not working.
Description: If you attempt to use the Software  mode of the TriggerType  property, the trigger is ignored, and data is acquired immediately when start  is called.
Workaround: There is no known workaround.

Summary: National Instruments NIDAQmx analog output devices ignore Default OutOfData mode.
Description: If you use the Default  setting of the OutOfData  property on a National Instruments analog output subsystem, it is ignored. It always provides Hold  behavior.
Workaround: Append a single additional value to the end of the generated data passed to putvalue containing the value that you want held after the generation is complete.

Summary: Stateflow sometimes centers subcharts incorrectly
Description: Stateflow's auto-centering feature (activated by pressing the space bar) sometimes fails.  This problem can occur when a transition emerges from one subchart and enters another subchart.  When both subcharts are visible on screen, Stateflow can miscalculate the center of the view.
Workaround: To improve auto-centering, make all subcharts a similar size and set them to similar zoom levels.

Summary: deconvwnr errors if the noise-to-signal argument (NSR) is the same size as the input image.
Description: deconvwnr  errors when the input image, I , and the noise-to-signal argument, NSR , are the same size, as in the syntax: deconvwnr(I,PSF,NSR) .
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the existing version of the deconvwnr.m file with the updated version within the attached file.Follow this procedure:Rename the deconvwnr.m file in your existing MATLAB installation directory, giving it a name such as deconvwnr.m.OLD. The file resides in this directory: MATLABROOT/toolbox/images/imageswhere MATLABROOT represents your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function.Download the attached file, extract deconvwnr.m, and copy it into the directory mentioned previously.Execute the following command at the MATLAB prompt: rehash toolbox

Summary: Issues with the Draw Shapes block when generating code for TI targets
Description: If you are generating code for a TI target and your model contains a Draw Shapes block, you might encounter errors when you try to generate code using Real-Time Workshop. These errors only occur if you set the Shapes parameter to Polygons and you selected the Fill shapes check box.
Workaround: 1. Unzip attachment_284623_20051216.zip. Place vipdrawshapes_rt.h, in the following directory:$matlabroot>\toolbox\vipblks\includewhere $matlabroot is the root MATLAB directory. This process will overwrite the existing vipdrawshapes_rt.h file in this directory.  2. The correct library files are included in the attached file, viplibs.zip. Download the zip file and extract it to the following directory: $matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic6000\tic6000\rtlib\This process will overwrite the existing .lib files in this directory.  You can unzip the file by typing the following commands at the MATLAB command prompt:>> outdir=fullfile(matlabroot,'toolbox','rtw','targets',...>> 'tic6000','tic6000','rtlib'); >> unzip('viplibs.zip',outdir) If you do not have permission to write to these directories, ask your system administrator for assistance.

Summary: MEX options files missing for Compaq Visual Fortran 6.1 and 6.6
Description: MEX options files for Compaq Visual Fortran 6.1 and 6.6 were inadvertently omitted from MATLAB 7.1 (R14SP3). As a result, mex -setup  does not include Compaq Visual Fortran 6.1 or 6.6 in the list of compilers to use, even if one of these compilers is installed.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the attached versions of the options files. Place the files in the following directory:$MATLAB\bin\win32\mexopts(where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.)Options files for the Compaq Visual Fortran compilers should then be available for use with mex.

Summary: Coverage reports for the Lookup Table block display incorrect information
Description: Coverage reports for the Lookup Table block display the following items incorrectly: The Coverage  data for the Look-up Table  metric identifies an incorrect value for total number of interpolation/extrapolation intervals. Under Look-up Table Details , the lookup table graphic positions the extrapolation cells incorrectly. If you click the rightmost extrapolation cell in the lookup table graphic, the resulting dialog displays an incorrect Interval  value.
Workaround: If you are using a previous version, there are no known workarounds at this time.

Summary: Custom Model Advisor checks fail to execute
Description: Custom Model Advisor checks fail to execute if the sl_customization.m  file that registers the custom checks resides in the root MATLAB directory or any of its subdirectories.
Workaround: Relocate your sl_customization.m file to a directory that is outside of the root MATLAB directory. You must add this directory to the MATLAB search path.Note that you can identify your root MATLAB directory by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: fetch Function Fails with Java Exception
Description: When retrieving data from a database using repeated fetch  statements, errors like the following might appear: ??? Java exception occurred: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space.Error in ==> cursor.fetch at 109       dataFetched = dataFetch(fet,resultSetMetaData,p.NullStringRead,p.NullNumberRead); or  Exception in thread "AWT-EventQueue-0" java.lang.OutOfMemoryError:  There is a bug in how Java memory is handled while using the fetch  function.
Workaround: Download the attached version of fetch by performing these steps:In the directory matlabroot/toolbox/database/database/@cursor(where matlabroot is the directory returned when you run the matlabroot function)rename the file fetch.m to fetchold.m.Quit MATLAB.Download the attached fetch.m file to matlabroot/toolbox/database/database/@cursorStart MATLAB.Run rehash toolboxcacheThe new version of fetch might perform more slowly than the previous version.If you try to retrieve large amounts of data with a single fetch statement, consider using the RowLimit argument to retrieve a specified number of rows at a time, which improves memory use and speed. For instructions, run doc fetch to see the reference page, or view the tutorial documentation at fetch (Database Toolbox) https://www.mathworks.com/access/helpdesk/help/toolbox/database/fetch.shtml

Summary: Maximum number of referenced models has reduced on Windows platforms
Description: The maximum number of referenced models supported by Simulink can be as few as 10 on Windows 2000 and about 200 on Windows/XP in R14SP3. This is significantly smaller than in previous releases. If your model references more than the maximum supported and the MATLAB working directory contains upper case characters, e.g., D:\Work, building the model's simulation or Real-Time Workshop target causes a compilation error similar to the following: The input line is too long.:getarg was unexpected at this time.gmake: *** [..\..\../mr_lat_aero_msf.mexw32] Error 0xff
Workaround: The number of models that can be referenced on Windows platforms is restricted by command line length limitations during the compilation and linking process. Given an average model name length of 25 characters, the R14SP2 limits on the number of submodels able to be referenced is approximately 50 on Windows 2000 and 400 on Windows XP. In R14SP3, these limits were further restricted due to an issue with file and path name case sensitivity.To restore the above R14SP2 limits on the maximum number of referenced models:Rename the file \toolbox\rtw\rtw\private\rtwinfomatman.p to rtwinfomatman.p_orig.Copy the attached file to this directory.Restart MATLAB.Remove or relocate the 'slprj' directory, it is located in the MATLAB pwd.

Summary: Scope and To Workspace blocks: double quote in block name might cause error in generated code
Description: If the name of a Scope or To Workspace block contains a double quote ("), Real-Time Workshop might generate code that cannot be compiled.
Workaround: Do one of the following:Remove double quotes from block names.Precede each double quote with a backslash (\). This marks the double quotes to be ignored.

Summary: FDATool errors for inverse-sinc lowpass filter design
Description: In Version 3.3 (R14sp3), FDATool generates a hard error when you select Inverse Sinc Lowpass  under Filter Type .  This error occurs only after you have stored a filter previously or loaded a saved filter or session.  This error also occurs if you launch FDATool from the Signal Processing Blockset's Digital Filter Design block.
Workaround: The attached p-code file setcomponentstate.p fixes this bug.  Download the file and place it in the following directory: MATLABROOT\toolbox\signal\sigtools\@siggui\@designpanel

Summary: S-function target: right-click S-function generation fails
Description: If all the following conditions exist, Real-Time Workshop can fail to generate code for an S-function:     You configure the model for the Real-Time Workshop S-function target.     The model includes a subsystem.     The subsystem includes a noninlined S-function.     Inside the subsystem, an output signal of the S-function connects to a Terminator block.     You use the right-click Generate S-Function  mechanism to generate the S-function.  The error that appears looks like the following: Error mymodel_sf.c: .\mymodel_mid.h: 217  unknown field `IndexSearch_o2' of `mymodel_BlockIO'
Workaround: To work around this bug, perform one of the following actions:        Move the Terminator block outside the subsystem from which Real-Time Workshop is generating the S-function.            Copy the attached file to your MATLAB installation tree as follows:    Note: In the following instructions, matlabroot represents your MATLAB installation root, as returned by the MATLAB command matlabroot.                    In your MATLAB installation tree, rename the following file, for example, by adding .old to the end of each file name.        matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/commonpass.tlc                        Download the attached file and place it in the same directory as the file you renamed.                Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Uninitialized variables can be generated for input ports connected to Demux or Signal Selector blocks
Description: Unpredictable behavior can result if the following conditions exist for a model:  The model includes an If block.  The model also includes either a Demux or a Signal Selector block.  The If block has more than one input port.  The Demux or Signal Selector block pulls scalar input from a wide signal source and feeds it into the If block.   Under these conditions, Real-Time Workshop generates uninitialized variables for the If block, which when used for the conditional expression can result in unpredictable behavior.
Workaround: The MathWorks strongly recommends that you avoid this bug by upgrading to a newer release.  Otherwise, the following workaround is available:Insert a Signal Conversion block with the following parameter settings between the Demux or Signal Selector block and the If block:Output set to Contiguous copyOverride optimizations and always copy signal selectedNote: To detect this condition, check the compiler diagnostic output. Most compilers produce a diagnostic when a variable is used before it is initialized. An example diagnostic is:mymodel.c(62) : warning C4700: local variable 'rtb_dbl_tmp' used without having been initialized

Summary: MLSEEQ may crash for certain parameters
Description: The MLSEEQ function, when invoked with a longer preamble length than the channel memory length, can cause a segmentation fault in MATLAB depending on the preamble parameter values.
Workaround: There are no existing workarounds.

Summary: Geodetic to Geocentric Latitude block gives incorrect results for large latitudes
Description: This block incorrectly computes the intermediate result for radius at a given latitude and thus produces incorrect results for large latitudes.
Workaround: Installation instructionsThe attached file contains a Geodetic to Geocentric Latitude block that has been updated to provide the correct output. To use the updated block, follow these steps:Log in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. On Linux systems, this is typically the root account.Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB>/toolbox/aeroblks/aeroblks/ folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file:/toolbox/aeroblks/aeroblks/aerolibtransform2.mdlTo see the contents of the .zip file: Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file and look at the contents.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh):unzip -l <MATLAB>/toolbox/aeroblks/aeroblks/<.zip file>Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB>/toolbox/aeroblks/aeroblks/ folder.Linux/Macintosh Systems: Type the following in a shell (Linux) or in a terminal window (Macintosh): cd <MATLAB>/toolbox/aeroblks/aeroblks/unzip <.zip file>Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB>/toolbox/aeroblks/aeroblks/ folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ˇ°WinZipˇ± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB>/toolbox/aeroblks/aeroblks/ folder.

Summary: Wrapverilog generated code can cause VHDL compilation errors for long port names
Description: VHDL code generated by the wrapverilog  TCL command can produce a ModelSim compiler error if any port names in the port list in the specified verilog module time have a length of more than 15 characters. The compiler error will resemble the following:
Workaround: To fix the problem, download the attached MATLAB file, vsim.m. Then place it in the following directory, overwriting the existing file: MATLABROOT\matlab\toolbox\modelsim\modelsim\

Summary: linprog large-scale function returns a vector that may not satisfy the bounds when both constraint matrices, A and Aeq, have a common zero column
Description: When using the linprog  function with the large-scale option and both constraint matrices, A  and Aeq , have a common zero column, the vector returned by linprog  may not satisfy the bounds.For example, if the ith column of [A; Aeq]  contains all zeros and the corresponding objective coefficient is f(i) = 0 , then linprog  returns a vector, x , where x(i)  may not satisfy the bounds on the variable lb(i)  and ub(i) .

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Incorrect code can be generated for masked, reusable subsystem when a Random Source block uses masked parameters
Description: Real-Time Workshop generates incorrect code if all of the following conditions exist for a model:  The model includes a reusable subsystem. The reusable subsystem uses masked parameters. The reusable subsystem includes a Random Source block from the Signal Processing Blockset. The Random Source block has the Repeatability  option set to Specify seed . The Random Source block uses the masked parameter as the setting for the Initial seed  parameter.  Under these conditions, Real-Time Workshop might inline the masked parameters when initializing the block instead of using the parameters of the initialization call. This results in code that looks like the following, where the rtp_noise_seed value is from a masked parameter: void sys_Start(rtDW_sys *localDW, uint32_T rtp_noise_seed) {   sys_Init(localDW, 67U);  /* ERROR: Inlining of seed value */} /* Start for root system: '<Root>' */ void MdlStart(void) {   /*enable Subsystem Block: <Root>/sys2 */   sys_Start(&rtDWork.sys2, 75U);  /* NOTE: Correct call with different seed value */  /*enable Subsystem Block: <Root>/sys1 */   sys_Start(&rtDWork.sys1, 67U); }
Workaround: The MathWorks strongly recommends that you avoid this bug by upgrading to a newer release.  Otherwise, the following workaround is available:Replace the file rtw/c/tlc/lib/syslib.tlc in your MATLAB installation area with the attached syslib.tlc file.Determine the MATLAB root directory on your machine by entering the MATLAB command matlabroot.Quit MATLAB.Rename the TLC file matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/c/tlc/lib/syslib.tlc to syslib.tlc.old, where matlabroot is the value returned in step 1.Download the attached file to the directory matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/c/tlc/lib. Restart MATLAB.Enter the MATLAB command rehash toolboxcache.

Summary: DAQREAD gives 'The data file cannot be read...Undefined function or variable 'num_of_blocks' when loading a .DAQ file.
Description: With larger .DAQ files, you may receive the message: ??? Error using ==> daqreadThe data file cannot be read. The following error occurred while reading the file:Undefined function or variable 'num_of_blocks'.
Workaround: You can correct the issue by modifying the daqread.m file in the directory$MATLAB/toolbox/matlab/iofun (where $MATLAB is your installation directory). You may need to make this file read/write before editing it.At approximately line 603 of daqread.m, you'll see the following:add_blocks = ceil(.25*(num_of_blocks));Change this to:add_blocks = ceil(.25*(blocks));

Summary: For certain modes of the Memoryless Nonlinearity block, the AM/PM characteristic wrongly depends on IIP3
Description: The Memoryless Nonlinearity block, in the RF Impairments library, can use two mathematical models of nonlinearities -- a cubic polynomial and a hyperbolic tangent. When these modes are chosen, the signal is scaled according to a function of the input third intercept point (IIP3). The AM/PM distortions are then applied to the scaled version of the signal. However, the AM/PM characteristic should not depend on the value of IIP3.

Summary: cvload fails for models that contain Stateflow charts with supertransitions
Description: The cvload  function fails to read coverage data from a file if the model that produced the coverage data contains a Stateflow chart with a supertransition ˇŞ i.e., a transition that crosses the boundary of a subchart. When invoking cvload  in this situation, Simulink reports the following error message in the MATLAB Command Window: The structure of model "TestSubChart1" is not consistent with the results in file "ctest.cvt" It will not be possible to fully map results back to the diagrams

Summary: xPC Target documentation incorrectly specifes support for Commtech Fastcom 422/2-PCI-335 board
Description: The xPC Target documentation incorrectly identifies the Commtech Fastcom 422/2-PCI-335 board as supported. The actual supported board is Commtech Fastcom 422/2-PCI.

Summary: Compiling an application that calls a MEX-function may seg fault
Description: Compiling a MATLAB based application that calls MEX-functions may cause a segmentation violation during compilation (during the execution of the mcc  command).This problem occurs on machines where the value of the TEMP  environment variable exceeds roughly 30 characters.
Workaround: Shorten the value of the TEMP environment variable. Set the environment variable TEMP to C:\temp. Make sure that C:\temp exists (create a new folder called temp on your C: drive if necessary).Alternately, the system can be patched to provide a permanent fix.  To apply this patch do the following:Download the patch zip file for your architecture.  Patched libraries are provided for win32, glnx86, glnxa64, mac and sol2 architectures.On development machine: Place the files for your platform into $matlab/bin/arch directory.Use the buildmcr command to create a new MCR redistributable.On the target machine (with a previously installed MCR): Place the files for your platform into the $mcrroot/v73/bin/arch directory.

Summary: Report Generator closes figures
Description: Report Generator sometimes closes Handle Graphics figure windows that are open when you generate a report. This happens because Report Generator does not correctly build the list of figures that are open.
Workaround: Add an Evaluate MATLAB Expression component at the beginning of the report. Enter these expressions to evaluate:set(rptgen_hg.appdata_hg,'PreRunOpenFigures',[]);get(rptgen_hg.appdata_hg,'PreRunOpenFigures');Clear the check boxes to insert the expression strings in the report and to display the command window output in the report.Note: The code in the workaround calls undocumented internal Report Generator functions. It is not guaranteed to be supported in future versions.

Summary: Speed input of the Asynchronous Machine SI Units block is normalized incorrectly
Description: Speed input of the SI version of the Asynchronous Machine block is normalized to 1 pu. For SI units, it should be defined in rad/s.

Summary: Integers: segmentation violation can occur when used with non-standard bit widths
Description: If a custom hardware implementation specifies integers with a bit width other than 8, 16, or 32 bits, a segmentation violation can occur before Real-Time Workshop generates code for the model.
Workaround: Use an integer bit width of 8, 16, or 32.

Summary: DM642 EVM model containing Video ADC or Video DAC blocks overruns
Description: Version 2.4 (R14SP3) introduced a task-based scheduler that uses a CPU timer operating at the highest rate in the Simulink model (the model base rate) to schedule events for all rates in the model. With the DM642 EVM target, the new task-based scheduler uses an external video clock to determine the time interval between successive video frames.The external source timing can drift relative to the CPU timing and this can result in the video frame-to-frame timing drifting relative to the CPU timer. The resulting apparent mismatch between the video ADC/DAC peripherals timing and the CPU timing can appear as an overrun condition for your running process on the target because the CPU timer governs the overrun status of your process.In this case, the overrun condition indicates that the real-time requirements for your process are not being satisfied relative to the CPU timer. The overrun may not mean that the data processing on the target is taking more time than allocated.
Workaround: To eliminate the overrun problem, follow these steps.Open the Configuration Options for your Simulink model.In the TIC6000 Code Generation options, set Overrun action to None or Notify_and_continue. This prevents your application from halting when it encounters an overrun.Save the new setting.Regenerate the code for your model with the new setting.Load the new code on to your DM642 EVM and rerun the program.

Summary: Certain FIG-file saved in R14SP2 can fail to load in R14SP3
Description: A MATLAB figure saved to a FIG-file in R14SP2 can fail to load in R14SP3, with an alert saying??? Error using ==> set Value must be a 4 element vector.  possibly followed by the error message Error in ==> hgload>figload_reset at 285       set(ax,'LooseInset',getappdata(ax,'LegendColorbarOriginalInset')); Error in ==> hgload at 158             figload_reset(h(k));  The bug only occurs when reading files for figures from which the legend or colorbar was deleted before saving. FIG-files that contain these objects do load properly unless an axes within them had a legend or colorbar at one point, but not when the figure was saved.
Workaround: There are two ways you can avoid the problem:Edit the MATLAB installed file toolbox/matlab/graphics/hgload.m; change line 282 from  if isappdata(ax,'LegendColorbarOriginalInset')to  if ~isempty(getappdata(ax,'LegendColorbarOriginalInset')) You can remove the appdata from the original FIG-file by loading it into R14SP2 and resaving to a new FIG-file.

Summary: Selecting customized Tools menu causes error
Description: Adding custom items to the Model Editor's Tools  menu and then selecting the menu causes the following error to appear at the MATLAB command line: ??? Undefined function or variable 'sl_action_schema'
Workaround: Replace every occurence of sl_action_schema in your menu customization code with DAStudio.ActionSchema. For example, replaceschema = sl_action_schema;withschema = DAStudio.ActionSchema;

Summary: Array Editor does not display multiline char arrays properly
Description: For variables containing multiline character arrays, the Array Editor only shows the first character in the array. For example x = ['Hello'; 'world']; appears as H  in the Array Editor.
Workaround: There is no way to view the variable properly in the Array Editor. To see its value, type the variable name in the Command Window and press Enter.

Summary: LOOPSYN errors out for plants with 1 output and 6+ inputs
Description: LOOPSYN errors out when its first input argument is an LTI model with one output and more than 6 inputs.
Workaround: To eliminate this error, replace the file    toolbox\robust\robust\@lti\private\istree.mby the attached version.

Summary: Issues occur when attaching Video and Image Processing Blockset generators to models using the Signal & Scope Manager
Description: If you try to attach the following blocks to a model using the Signal & Scope Manager, the block dialog box is not formatted properly:From Multimedia FileImage From FileRead AVI FileRead Binary FileAlso, you get the following warning message at the MATLAB command line:Warning: Attempt to reference field of non-structure array.
Workaround: Access these blocks using the Simulink Library Browser or the Video and Image Processing Blockset library. You can open this library by typing viplib at the MATLAB command line.

Summary: Load of ASCII file slow on Mac OS X
Description: Loading of an ASCII text file using the load  command with the -ascii  flag has slowed down considerably in MATLAB R14sp3 when run under Mac OS X.
Workaround: Under MATLAB R14sp3, for most ASCII files, you can use either the textscan or dlmread function to read the text efficiently.

Summary: MATLAB Code generation error with colormaps or other large initialized arrays
Description: When MATLAB generates MATLAB code for figure windows, errors can result when large arrays are part of the generated code. For example, the commands  image colormap('winter') result in an error dialog that says ERRSTR  when you select Generate MATLAB File  from the File  menu. This error will also occur when you specify large non-standard colormaps or initialize any large numeric arrays.

Summary: New RTF stylesheets saved with wrong format
Description: The Report Generator stylesheet editor enables custom editing of document stylesheets. When you create a new stylesheet with the New DSSSL (RTF)  stylesheet option, Report Generator saves the stylesheet in an invalid format, and the stylesheet is unusable.
Workaround: Instead of creating a new RTF stylesheet, edit an existing one.  Under the DSSSL Print (RTF) category, edit the Standard Print stylesheet.  This opens a copy of the Standard Print stylesheet, which has default options similar to those of a newly created RTF stylesheet.

Summary: Model with Nx1-dimension signal and Embedded Matlab block that accesses vector as u(i,1) fails to update
Description: I have a Simulink model with an Inport block of port dimension N-by-1 feeding an Embedded MATLAB Function block. MATLAB hangs when I reference this input from within the Embedded MATLAB Function block using two indices (i,1) , and either update the diagram or simulate the model. I reference the input as follows:u(i,1) This model worked fine in earlier versions of MATLAB.
Workaround: To work around this issue, reference the N-by-1 matrix signal, which is essentially an N-length vector, as follows:u(i)Alternatively, you can flatten the N-by-1 signal in Simulink to a vector of N elements.

Summary: Colorbars and legends follow axes when panning/zooming/rotating in 3D
Description: A colorbar or legend attached to a 3-D plot will by default resize and move when the view is manipulated to maintain a consistent size and location relative to the plot axes. This behavior occurs for any of the automatic layout options that the Location  property establishes for a colorbar or legend.
Workaround: Set the colorbar or legend Location property to Manual by opening the Property Editor, selecting the colorbar or legend, and selecting the Manual radio button in the Property Editor.

Summary: Generated code fails to compile due to name collisions between constant data and local data
Description: The generated code from Stateflow fails to compile when the following conditions exist:The model contains two charts with identically named data, one declared with Local  scope and the other declared with Constant  scope. These charts could also belong to multiple library models, but contain links that are used in the same main model.  The model uses ERT target to generate code. The Symbol Format  option does not use the model name to prefix the identifiers. For example, the symbol format string appears as $N$M   instead of $R$N$M  .  In this scenario, assume that the local data item and constant data item are each named x . The generated code contains a #define   for the symbol x  to implement the constant data as follows:/* Named constants for block: '/Chart1' */#define x    (0.0) The symbol x  also appears as a field in the DWork data structure to implement the local data. During compilation, the C pre-processor replaces all instances of x  with the constant value, resulting in compilation errors on seemingly innocuous lines, as in this example:    rtDWork.Chart.x++;name_bug_main.c(58) : error C2059: syntax error : '(' name_bug_main.c(59) : error C2059: syntax error : '(' name_bug_main.c(102) : error C2059: syntax error : '('
Workaround: Change the name of one of the data objects or change the symbol format to use the model name as a prefix.

Summary: FDATool generates incorrect MATLAB file code for FIR constrained equiripple filter designs
Description: When you generate code for FIR constrained equiripple filter designs in FDATool, the code generated by FDATool passes the input arguments isincffactor  and isincpower  to firceqrip  as separate arguments. This causes firceqrip  to return an error when you try to use the MATLAB file code to filter a signal.The arguments isincffactor  and isincpower  need to be passed as a single argument grouped in square brackets.
Workaround: Work around this by modifying the generated code.  Replace the string isincffactor, isincpower with [isincffactor isincpower] in the generated code input for the firceqrip function.

Summary: Seg-fault using slcopy_mdl2subsys.m
Description: Models with unconnected Signal & Scope Manager viewers seg-fault when using the slcopy_mdl2subsys.m utility.
Workaround: Running the attached MATLAB file will let the user know if slcopy_mdl2subsys.m is going to fail due to unconnected Signal & Scope Manager viewers.  The unconnected Signal & Scope Manager viewers need to be removed or connected before running slcopy_mdl2subsys.m.

Summary: Compiler-generated application fails to run on MAC OS X 10.4
Description: When running a Compiler-generated application on MAC OS X 10.4, the application fails with the error:Fatal error loading library /devel/R14sp3d/perfect/matlab/bin/mac/libmwmclmcr.dylib Error: dlopen(/devel/R14sp3d/perfect/matlab/bin/mac/libmwmclmcr.dylib, 10): Symbol not found: __XEditResPutWidgetInfo   Referenced from: /devel/R14sp3d/perfect/matlab/sys/os/mac/libXm.2.dylib   Expected in: flat namespace This bug was caused by changes in the dynamic library loader in MAC OS X 10.4.
Workaround: 1.  Delete the file:  $MATLAB/bin/mac/libmwmclmcrrt.dylib  (where $MATLAB = the MATLAB root directory on your machine)2.  Download the two files (attached below):  libmwmclmcrrt.dylib.7.3  libmwmclmcrrt.dylib.73.csf3.  Place the files from Step 2 in the directory:  $MATLAB/bin/mac4.  Create a soft link to libmwmclmcrrt.dylib.7.3 by issuing the following command at the system terminal prompt:ln -s libmwmclmcrrt.dylib.7.3 libmwmclmcrrt.dylib 5. Issue the following command at the MATLAB command prompt:buildmcr6. Rebuild your application.

Summary: The quantize function throws erroneous warnings
Description: The quantizer  object's quantize  function throws bogus empty warnings when no overflow has occurred. For example:  warning backtrace on q = quantizer; quantize(q,0);Warning:  In \\Bat13\Adsp\nightly\matlab\toolbox\fixedpoint\fixedpoint\@embedded\@quantizer\quantize.p>quantize at 71
Workaround: To work around this problem,   1. Quit MATLAB   2. Rename the following files by appending a .old extension. For example, rename files to quantize.p.old and unitquantize.p.old:   Windows  $MATLAB\toolbox\fixedpoint\fixedpoint\@embedded\@quantizer\quantize.p  $MATLAB\toolbox\fixedpoint\fixedpoint\@embedded\@quantizer\unitquantize.p  All other platforms $MATLAB/toolbox/fixedpoint/fixedpoint/@embedded/@quantizer/quantize.p  $MATLAB/toolbox/fixedpoint/fixedpoint/@embedded/@quantizer/unitquantize.p  3. Download the zip file in the Attachments section below, and extract files quantize.p and unitquantize.p from it.  Place files quantize.p and unitquantize.p in  $MATLAB/toolbox/fixedpoint/fixedpoint/@embedded/@quantizer/  which is the same directory mentioned in Step 2.   4. Restart MATLAB

Summary: Debugging causes Workspace browser, Array Editor, and Current Directory browser to show incorrect information
Description: When debugging a MATLAB file, the Workspace browser and Array Editor might not refresh their content. Also while debugging, the Current Directory browser might not show the correct current directory. For example, this occurs when the MATLAB file you are debugging contains a function that changes the current directory. After quitting debugging, the Current Directory browser still does not show the correct current directory.
Workaround: You can use a temporary workaround, or download a patch to resolve the problem.As a temporary workaround for the Workspace browser and Array Editor, open and then close a figure window. The Workspace browser and Array Editor update correctly for the rest of that MATLAB session. To make this happen automatically, add this statement to your startup.m file: figure; closeAnother temporary workaround is to select an empty area in the Workspace browser, right-click, and select Refreshfrom the context menu. This updates the values in the Workspace browser, but not in the Array Editor. To see the current value of a variable in the Array Editor, close the variable in the Array Editor and then reopen it.As a temporary workaround for the Current Directory browser, click the Go up one level button on the toolbar. The Current Directory browser then shows the correct current directory.For a permanent workaround, download and use the attached patch file by performing these steps:Quit MATLAB.Download the attachment patch278496.zip to matlabroot\java (where matlabroot is the directory returned when you run the matlabroot function).Start MATLAB.In MATLAB, run these statements:        cd([matlabroot '/java'])       mkdir('patch')       unzip('patch278496.zip')Quit MATLAB.Start MATLAB and the problem should be resolved.There might still be some values in the Workspace browser or Array Editor that appear blank or show Error. That aspect of the problem should be resolved with the next version of MATLAB.

Summary: Accessing simulation time in the default transitions of charts with execute-at-initialization enabled may yield incorrect results
Description: In charts that enable the execute-at-initialization  property, accessing simulation time via the implicit variable t   in the default transition paths may yield incorrect results. This is because the internal variable used by Stateflow to keep track of the simulation time is not reset to 0  during model initialization time. As a result, the default transitions that get executed in these charts during model initialization may access stale values stored in the simulation time variable.
Workaround: Do not access simulation time in the default transition paths that get executed during model initialization.

Summary: Environment Controller block behaves incorrectly in a referenced model
Description: When used in a referenced model, an Environment Controller block chooses the RTW path regardless of whether the top model is being simulated or used to generate code.

Summary: multibandread fails when arguments specify larger dataset that the file contains
Description: Passing multibandread  a file with less data than specified in the arguments returns the following unhelpful error message: ??? Error using ==> memmapfile.memmapfile>subsrefA subscripting operation on the Data field attempted to create acomma-separated list. The memmapfile class does not support the use ofcomma-separated lists when subscripting.Error in ==> multibandread>readMemFile at 154im = m.data.x(ndx{1}, ndx{2}, ndx{3});Error in ==> multibandread at 131    im = readMemFile(filename, info, ndx, readOrder);
Workaround: This failure occurs because the combination of data dimensions, offset, and datatype arguments passed to multibandread specify a larger dataset than what the file actually contains. To avoid this error, specify arguments that match the data in the file.

Summary: C24x ADC and C28x ADC blocks do not work when the target is configured to use internal memory
Description: When the LinkerCMDFile  option in the target preferences block is set to Internal_memory_map , the linker command file does not correctly allocate the ramfuncs  memory section. The ramfuncs  section is defined in Texas Instruments peripheral header/example files for ADC. The problem is caused by the incorrect use of the DSECT  attribute in the ramfuncs  section making it ovelap with other code or data in memory.
Workaround: Open the generated linker command file and remove the invalid DSECT attribute from the ramfuncs memory section.For a more detailed discussion of the LinkerCMDFile option, see the reference pages on the LF2407 eZdsp and the F2812 eZdsp target preferences blocks in the Embedded Target for the TI C2000 DSP documentation.

Summary: Mixed rising/falling edge clocks in the VHDL Cosimulation block may cause erroneous ModelSim clock waveform displays
Description: When mixed rising and falling edge clock signals are specified in the Clocks  pane of the VHDL Cosimulation block, the corresponding clock waveforms in ModelSim may be displayed incorrectly. Falling edge clocks may be displayed as rising edge, or vice versa. This problem does not occur if the rising/falling edge property is set identically for all clocks (for example if all clocks are rising edge).

Summary: Stateflow functions: compiler error generated when model includes an Integrator block and a Stateflow chart that includes a noninlined function
Description: If all of the following conditions exist in a model, Real-Time Workshop generates a compiler error in the Stateflow function indicating that a task identifier (tid ) is not declared:  The model includes a block that has a continuous state, such as an Integrator block.  The model includes a non-empty Stateflow chart and generates non-inlined stateflow code.  The model is configured with the system target file rsim.tlc  or rtwsfcn.tlc

Summary: Segmentation Violation error when repositioning clock entries in the VHDL Cosimulation block
Description: A Segmentation Violation error occurs, or an error message is displayed,  when the Move Up  button in the Clocks  pane of the VHDL Cosimulation block is clicked to reposition a clock signal in the list.
Workaround: To fix the problem, download the attached P-file, moveClockRow.p. Then place it in the following directory overwriting the existing file: MATLABROOT\matlab\toolbox\modelsim\modelsim\@modelsimddg\@CoSim\

Summary: Notebook returns stopEverythingIfBadRegistry error
Description: Notebook returns the following error for some configurations: ??? Error using ==> notebook>stopEverythingIfBadRegistryWord not properly installed: registry entry not found for template locationError in ==> notebook at 31stopEverythingIfBadRegistry This error occurs on PCs with a Microsoft Word or Office template that has a non-standard or non-English language name or location.
Workaround: If you are using R14SP3, replace the existing MATLAB? notebook.m with the attached version by performing these steps:In MATLAB, identify the location of your existing notebook.m file. It is in matlabroot\toolbox\matlab\codetools, where matlabroot is the directory returned when you type matlabroot in the MATLAB Command Window.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached zip file and unzip it to find the corrected notebook.m file.Copy the new notebook.m to matlabroot\toolbox\matlab\codetools, replacing the old version.Start MATLAB.  Notebook should run without errors.

Summary: Updating sample times of a Level-2 MATLAB file S-function can fail
Description: Updating a model can fail to update the sample times of a Level-2 MATLAB file S-function in the following case: The S-function uses port-based sample times. The S-function specifies its sample times as inherited. You have previously updated (or simulated) the model in which the S-function resides.  In this case, Simulink fails to update the S-function's sample times when you update the model again. As a result, one or more of the S-function's sample times will be incorrect if you have changed the model in such a way as to change any of the sample times that the S-function should inherit. This, in turn, can cause simulating the model to produce incorrect results.

Summary: Macintosh OS 10.4.2 and Later: MATLAB Can Become Non-Responsive, Terminate, or Cause Other Problems
Description: On Mac OS 10.4.2 or later, certain operations can cause MATLAB to become non-responsive or terminate unexpectedly, or can result in failures in Macintosh system preferences. All of these problems are due to the libTIFF.dylib  library included with MATLAB. Specific actions that cause this problem are Printing from the Editor/Debugger  Selecting Edit > Copy Figure  Running the sound  Function Opening Macintosh System Preferences    Printing from the Editor/Debugger and Selecting Edit > Copy Figure  When you print from the MATLAB Editor/Debugger or select Edit > Copy Figure , either nothing happens or MATLAB can crash or become non-responsive. The message associated with this problem (shown below) is only visible by opening the Console application, or is visible if MATLAB is started from a terminal window. To view this error message, start MATLAB with the -nodesktop  option, or open the Console application, located in the Utilities folder of the Applications folder. 2005-09-20 15:07:20.787 MATLAB[1584] CFLog (21): Error loading /System/Library/Frameworks/Carbon.framework/Versions/A/Frameworks/Print.framework/Versions/Current/Plugins/PrintCocoaUI.bundle/Contents/MacOS/PrintCocoaUI:  error code 4, error number 0 (Symbol not found:  __cg_TIFFWriteDirectory  Referenced from: /System/Library/Frameworks/ApplicationServices.framework/Versions/A/Frameworks/ImageIO.framework/Versions/A/ImageIO  Expected in: /Applications/MATLAB7/bin/mac/libTIFF.dylib  The exact path to /bin/mac/libTIFF.dylib  reported in the log depends the version of MATLAB installed and the location. Other details depend on which MATLAB feature loads the library. Running the sound Function  Running sound.m  reports an error similar to??? Couldn't create new sound channel (-2004).Error in ==> sound at 41playsnd(y,fs,bits); Opening Macintosh System Preferences  Opening System Preferences from the Apple menu crashes System Preferences. Underlying Cause The cause of these problems is that the libTIFF.dylib  library provided with MATLAB conflicts with the version of this library used by Mac OS 10.4.2 and later. When you start MATLAB, the MATLAB version of the libtiff.dylib  library is loaded into memory. When you try to print, copy figures, play sound, or access System Preferences, the Macintosh operating system calls the MATLAB version of this library, and certain symbols the operating system expects to find are not defined.
Workaround: To work around this problem, remove the libtiff.dylib from MATLAB.Specifically, close MATLAB. Then open the Mac OS Terminal application (or X11) and type the following:cd matlabroot/bin/macrm libtiff.dylib

Summary: Segmentation violation during code generation for Stateflow charts containing local events and/or recursive functions
Description: Stateflow models containing event broadcasts or recursive functions may sometimes trigger a segmentation fault error in one of the code generation optimizations. Below is an example of what the top of the stack trace looks like when the error is triggered. Stack Trace:  [0] cg_ir.dll:public: bool __thiscall CG::Set >::addAll(class CG::Set > *)(0x165f9c60, 0, 0x168d3410, 0x00ccbf30) + 48 bytes  [1] cg_ir.dll:private: void __thiscall CG::ModRefAnalysis::computeCallModRef (class CG_Node *,int,bool,class CG::FunctionModRefInfo *)(0x168d3410, 0, 0, 0x16977018) + 428 bytes  [2] cg_ir.dll:private: void __thiscall CG::ModRefAnalysis::computeExpressionModRef(class CG_Node *,int,bool,class CG::FunctionModRefInfo*)(0x168d3410, 0, 0, 0x16977018) + 305 bytes  [3] cg_ir.dll:private: void __thiscall CG::ModRefAnalysis::computeExpressionModRef(class CG_Node *,int,bool,class CG::FunctionModRefInfo *)(0x168c49b8, 0, 1, 0x16977018)+ 128 bytes  [4] cg_ir.dll:private: void __thiscall CG::ModRefAnalysis::computeExprStmtModRef(class CG_Node *,class CG::FunctionModRefInfo *)(0x168c49b8, 0x16977018, 0x1690c5a8, 0x168c49b8) + 58 bytes  [5] cg_ir.dll:private: void __thiscall CG::ModRefAnalysis::computeStatementModRef(class CG_Node *,class CG::FunctionModRefInfo *)(0x168c49b8, 0x16977018, 0x1690b770, 0x165f9240) + 55 bytes  [6] cg_ir.dll:private: void __thiscall CG::ModRefAnalysis::computeModRefFixpoint(void)(0x165f9240, 0x16668f30, 0x164acfd8, 0x0f49cb40) + 139 bytes
Workaround: Type the following command in MATLAB to turn off the optimization prior to using Stateflow: sf('feature', 'Minimize Copies on Calls','off');

Summary: imfilter returns incorrect result when filter is separable
Description: The imfilter  function returns results that are incorrectly reflected or shifted when the filter is separable.
Workaround: To workaround this issue, edit the file toolbox/images/images/imfilter.m in your MATLAB installation and replace the following line: hrow = conj(v(:,1))' * sqrt(s(1));with this line: hrow = v(:,1)' * sqrt(s(1));

Summary: Startup exception sometimes appears for MATLAB
Description: Upon starting MATLAB, a dialog box sometimes appears displaying the following error message: java.lang.NullPointerException at com.mathworks.mwswing.desk.DTNestingContainer$TreeState.findSubTree(DTNestingContainer.java:1840) at com.mathworks.mwswing.desk.DTNestingContainer.getPreviousLocation(DTNestingContainer.java:765) . . . MATLAB then exits. This occurs if during the previous MATLAB session you closed or undocked all the tools within the main desktop frame, including the Command Window, and then exited MATLAB.
Workaround: Delete the file MATLABDesktop.xml from the MATLAB preferences directory (the directory returned when you run prefdir). To avoid recurrence, keep one tool open in the desktop before exiting MATLAB.

Summary: rtwbuild will fail when case used in model name is not exact match
Description: The rtwbuild  or slbuild  command generates an error if the following conditions exist: You initiate the command on a Windows platform. The character casing specified in the model name does not match the casing in the name of the model you are trying to build.
Workaround: Reenter the command, making sure the case used for characters in the model name you specify matches that of the actual model name.

Summary: Gaussian window returns incorrect results.
Description: The Gaussian window (gausswin ) gives incorrect results because the exponent is being divided by the window length rather than the window order. This error is particularly noticeble for short windows.

Summary: Why can't I put the Video Input block into a subsystem?
Description: When I try to put the Video Input  block into a subsystem, I receive the following error: Error in 'imaqvip/Subsystem/Video Input': Initialization commands cannot be evaluated.  MATLAB error message: Error using ==> get_paramSubSystem block does not have a parameter named 'BlockDiagramType'.
Workaround: Installation instructionsLog in to an account with appropriate permissions to overwrite files in your MATLAB installation. Locate your MATLAB installation by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt. This folder is referred to as <MATLAB> in subsequent steps.Exit MATLAB.Download the attached .zip file associated with this Bug Report and store the file in the <MATLAB> folder.Make a backup copy of each of the files listed in the .zip file.Extract the files from the .zip file. Windows Systems*: Open the .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Delete the .zip file in the <MATLAB> folder.*Notes for Windows Systems:If you see ˇ°Error: Access is deniedˇ± when attempting to extract the .zip file, you may need to start ˇ°WinZipˇ± in administrator mode. Do this by navigating to WinZip.exe or the WinZip Start menu entry, right-clicking on it, and selecting ˇ°Run as administratorˇ±.From within WinZip, find and open the downloaded .zip file.Extract the contents of the file to your <MATLAB> folder.Change log:July 13, 2011: Converted stand-alone file to a .zip file attachment.

Summary: CAN-AC2-104 fifo setup
Description: The CAN-AC2-104 FIFO Setup block crashes the kernel if the Termination  struct is used.
Workaround: Quit MATLAB. Unzip the attached file and replace the existing canac2104setupf.c in the $MATLAB\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks directory with the new file(where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by entering matlabrootin the MATLAB Command Window.) Restart MATLAB. Enter the following in the MATLAB Command Window:  rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Assignment block in iterator systems can cause unexpected results
Description: If an Assignment block is used in an iterator system, Simulink copies the data from the first input port to the the output port on the first iteration only.  If Simulink determines that the entire signal will be overwritten during an iteration, Simulink, as an optimization, does not perform the copy.  Simulink determines that the copy is not needed in the following circumstances: The Assignment block is in vector mode and the source of element indices is external.  The iterator system is a For Iterator system.   The output of the For Iterator block is directly connected to the element indices port.  The number of iterations in the For Iterator system is internal and is equal to the width of the output port of the Assignment block.   The Assignment block does not reside in a conditionally executed subsystem.   The For Iterator block does not use the set next iteration variable externally  option.   This optimization can cause unexpected results if the model assumes that the copy happens.
Workaround: Ensure that the model fails to meet at least one of the conditions described in the bug description. For example, use a While Iterator block instead of a For Iterator block or make the number of iterations in the For Iterator block external.

Summary: Logging of Parameters in SimBiology
Description: Parameters in SimBiology are logged only if StatesToLog  is set to all .
Workaround: To log parameters in SimBiology set StatesToLog to all.

Summary: Nested Switch blocks can return incorrect linearization results
Description: Models will linearize incorrectly when  There are two switches on a single branch of a system (i.e. the output of one switch block directly or indirectly feeds the input of another switch block), and The dimension of the output of the second switch is greater than one, and Conditional input branch execution  is turned on.
Workaround: To work around this problem, disable Conditional input branch execution for the model. This option is located in the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog.

Summary: Block reduction: Simulink Accelerator fails to generate code with block reduction optimization enabled
Description: Simulink Accelerator fails to generate code if the following conditions exist:  The model includes two identical atomic (nonvirtual) subsystems.  The RTW system code  option on the Block Parameter dialog box for the two subsystems is set to Auto .  One of the subsystems is connected to a Terminator block, while the other subsystem is connected to a block other than a Terminator block.  The Block reduction optimization  option on the Optimization  pane of the Configuration Parameter is selected.  This bug exists also in the previous product, Simulink Performance Tools, which contains Simulink Accelerator functionality.
Workaround: Disable the Block reduction optimization option on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Incorrect percentages reported for 'Look-up Table' metric
Description: When generating N-Dimensional Lookup Table Reports, model coverage displays incorrect Coverage  data for the Look-up Table  metric.
