For example, when using a 10-by-10 Lookup Table (2-D) block, model coverage falsely identifies 143 total interpolation/extrapolation intervals. In this case, model coverage should instead report only 121 total intervals.

Summary: In an Embedded MATLAB Function Block, a fixed-point function reports an internal error
Description: There is a known problem with name resolution that may cause fixed-point library functions to report internal errors in the Embedded MATLAB Function Block. This bug is triggered by particular call sequences of fixed-point functions. Calling the .*  operator followed by reshape  is one example that triggers the bug.The name resolution issue is the likely cause of the errors if any of the following conditions is true: Applying the workaround resolves the problem. The first error message refers to an invalid handle . The top-most error message starts with function call failed  and refers to one of these operators:
Workaround: Replace the MATLAB files in /toolbox/eml/lib/fixedpoint/@embedded/@fi with the attached MATLAB files of the same names.Use the attached MATLAB files with R14sp3 only.

Summary: Simulink Math Function block gives incorrect frame status for transpose and hermitian modes
Description: For frame-based inputs, the outputs of a transpose or hermitian operation should always be sample-based. Frame-based multichannel signals are represented as matrices. An M-by-N frame-based matrix represents M consecutive samples from each of N independent channels. After a transpose or hermitian operation, the output is N-by-M, and we cannot say it is still frame-based with M channels, N samples per channel. However, the Math Function block is incorrectly reporting the frame status as frame-based after a transpose or hermetian operation.

Summary: Why do I receive unexpected results using the zoom command with a factor argument?
Description: If I run the commandsplot(0:100,0:100)zoom(2)zoom(2) from the MATLAB command window, I receive axes limits that are different from running the commandsplot(0:100,0:100)zoom(4) I would expect these two commands to yield the same results. The commandsplot(0:100,0:100)zoom(4)zoom(0.5) andplot(0:100,0:100)zoom(4)zoom(0.25) also appear to provide the same results, although according to the documentation one should zoom out by a factor of 2 and the other should zoom out by a factor of 4. In fact, all zoom factors greater than zero and less than one zoom out by 0.5.
Workaround: A bug in MATLAB 6.5 (R13) causes incorrect results in the axes limits of figures when the zoom command is issued with a zoom factor as an argument. No workarounds exist.

Summary: Generated C code might fail to compile for Stateflow blocks that use graphical subfunctions
Description: If a Stateflow block uses graphical sub-functions, and Real-Time Workshop generates C code for the S-Function or Real-Time Simulation (RSIM) target, the code for the block may fail to compile and give the following error message:     undeclared identifier 'tid'
Workaround: Download the attached zip file and extract the file utillib.tlc. Use this file to replace the following file:     MATLABROOT/rtw/c/tlc/lib/utillib.tlc

Summary: cp2tform outputs Qhull error
Description: cp2tform  outputs a very long error message from Qhull. If you follow the instructions given in this error message, you can get cp2tform  to run, but you still get a warning message from Qhull.
Workaround: Replace the version of cp2tform in your existing MATLAB installation with the updated version contained in the attachment. Save toolbox/images/images/cp2tform.m to toolbox/images/images/cp2tform.original.Download the attached file, extract cp2tform.m and place it in the directory above.

Summary: cp2tform and imtransform computation error with 'piecewise linear' extrapolation
Description: There is a bug in the way cp2tform  and imtransform  compute some pixels outside the convex hull of a triangulation when the transform type is set to 'piecewise linear' .

Summary: Large scale version of fmincon generates an error when a user-defined output function is passed.
Description: When a user-defined output function is passed to the large scale version of the fmincon  function via the OutputFcn  option, fmincon  generates an error message similar to the following:  ??? Attempt to reference field of non-structure array.Error in ==> sfminbx at 342            stop = feval(outputfcn,[],[],'interrupt',varargin{:});Error in ==> fmincon at 536      [X,FVAL,LAMBDA,EXITFLAG,OUTPUT,GRAD,HESSIAN] = ...

Summary: UEI boards only work in single-ended input mode
Description: The United Electronic Industries (UEI) A/D sample-based blocks do not allow you to specify single-ended or differential input. They only allow single-ended input.
Workaround: If you need to use differential input with the sample based UEI blocks, contact The Mathworks Technical Support for a driver that will only work in differential mode. The current product (released) driver only works in single-ended mode.

Summary: SegV when Port Values are visible
Description: A SegV may result when Port Values  are set to either Show When Hovering  or Toggle When Clicked  and when the block whose values are shown is removed from the block diagram.
Workaround: Leave the Port Values setting to Show None.

Summary: Invalid uigetdir syntax in documentation
Description: The uigetdir  reference page incorrectly includes the following syntax for use on UNIX platforms only.directory_name = uigetdir('start_path','dialog_title',x,y).  where x  and y  specify the initial location of the dialog box. This is not a valid syntax. uigetdir  does not accept arguments for location co-ordinates on any platform.

Summary: Cannot save library models that contain System Requirements blocks
Description: Suppose a library block contains a System Requirements block. To generate an example:Create a new library. Add a Subsystem block to the library. Within the Subsystem, add a System Requirements block. Save the library.   When copying the library block to a model, Simulink reports the following warning in the MATLAB Command Window: Warning: Error evaluating 'CopyFcn' callback of System Requirements block (mask) 'untitled/Subsystem/System Requirements'. Error using ==> set_paramAttempting to override parameters of 'untitled/Subsystem/System Requirements' which is inside a library link.  When attempting to save the model, Simulink reports another warning in the MATLAB Command Window: Warning: Error evaluating 'PostSaveFcn' callback of System Requirements block (mask) 'untitled/Subsystem/System Requirements'. Index exceeds matrix dimensions.  Also, Simulink displays the following error message: An error or warning occurred during a callback while saving 'C:\\modelname.mdl'. The previously saved version of this file (if any) has not been changed.  In this situation, Simulink fails to save the model.

Summary: Small output voltage errors with National Instruments PCI-607x boards
Description: The analog output voltage range for the National Instruments PCI-607x boards is incorrectly specified as -10V to +10V in the driver, whereas the correct values are -10.24V to +10.24V. This causes analog output voltage errors up to 2.4% of the full range.

Summary: Support for DOORS Version 8
Description: Upgrading to DOORS Version 8 from an earlier DOORS release after the Requirements Management Interface (RMI) has been installed can cause errors when executing the rmi setup  script again. You will see messages similar to: (dmiSurrogateM_) already declared in this scope   Included from:  ...
Workaround: This problem results from the DOORS upgrade script copying MATLAB extensions into a new file that is not visible to MATLAB's DOORS interface installer.To work around this issue, manually comment out the dmi content from the copiedFromDoors7.dxl file as follows:Navigate to Telelogic\DOORS_8.0\lib\dxl\startupFiles.Open the copiedFromDoors7.dxl file with a text editor.Comment out the line:#include <addins/dmi/dmi.inc>It should now look like this://#include <addins/dmi/dmi.inc>Save and close the file.Start DOORS and MATLAB.Run the setup script by typing the following at the MATLAB prompt:rmi setup

Summary: A root Inport bus can cause a dimensions propagation error
Description: A root-level Inport block that specifies bus objects can cause a dimensions propagation error under the following conditions: The bus signal from the root-level Inport contains subbus signals. A Bus Selector selects one of these subbus signals. The selected signal is connected to a Bus Assignment block.  If you update or simulate a model under these conditions, Simulink displays an error message similar to the following:Invalid dimensions encountered while propagating dimensions from output port 1 of 'ShowsProblem/Controller2Plant' to input port 1 of 'ShowsProblem/Bus Selector2'. During propagation, we are attempting to unify the dimensions of the multiplexed signal with dimensions {1,1,1,1,1,1} to obtain the dimensions 1.   This error message is indirectly related to a hidden SignalConversion block. In this example, this block is added for block ShowsProblem/Controller2Plant  at output port 1 as result of block insertion or expansion. The hidden block's parameter Output is set to Virtual bus . Consider manually inserting such a block to debug the problem.
Workaround: The following workarounds are available for this issue:Set the root Inport to output a nonvirtual bus. This can be done on the Signal Specification pane of the Inport block's parameter dialog box.After breaking signals out of the bus with a Bus Selector, perform an operation (e.g. add a Gain), or place a Signal Conversion (in the Signal Attributes library) on the signal. Set the Signal Conversion to Contiguous Copy.  This breaks the back propagation of signal dimensions that lead to this error.

Summary: Ignore custom storage classes option is selected when the Target Preferences block is added to new model
Description: With Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder installed on a machine, dragging a TIC6000 Target Preferences block to a new Simulink model configures the model for ERT and selects the Ignore custom storage classes  option by default.Ignore custom storage classes  is cleared when the model is configured for ert.tlc , for example if the model is not configured for TIC6000 ti_c6000_ert.tlc .
Workaround: After you generate a new model, select Simulation>Configuration Parameters>Real-Time Workshop and clear the Ignore custom storage classes option.

Summary: MATLAB Compiler generated applications can't run in the background.
Description: Software components generated by the MATLAB Compiler and running in the background are quickly suspended by the operating system and hang until killed. This includes main programs, C or C++ shared libraries, and all components generated by products that are MATLAB Compiler extensions, such as MATLAB Builder for .NET and MATLAB Builder for Excel. The hang occurs because components generated by the MATLAB Compiler attempt to access the terminal. (All POSIX-compilant operating systems issue a SIGTTOU signal to background processes that attempt to access the terminal. The default action taken on receipt of SIGTTOU is to suspend the process.)
Workaround: To work around this problem (for versions prior to 2006a), modify the output of the compiler to block the SIGTTOU signal and rebuild your component.On UNIX systems, you need to insert this code into your main routine:    struct sigaction block;    block.sa_handler = SIG_IGN;    sigaction(SIGTTOU, &block, NULL);    sigaction(SIGTTIN, &block, NULL);How and where you insert it depends on what kind of a main routine you have. Typically, you will either have manually written a main routine yourself, in which you're calling mclInitializeComponentInstance(), or you will have used the MATLAB Compiler to automatically generate a main routine for you.If you have manually written your own main routine, place the block of code listed above in your main function, before you make any calls to any MATLAB or MATLAB Compiler related functions (such as mclInitializeComponentInstance, mclInitializeApplication or any of the mx routines used to create MATLAB matrix data).If you are using the MATLAB Compiler to automatically generate a main routine, follow these steps:Compile using the verbose option, as shown: mcc -mv <your MATLAB files>Using -v (verbose) when you run mcc generates output that displays the MBUILD command that you need to rebuild your component.Examine the output for the MBUILD command.Edit the generated *_main.c file. Place the C/C++ code listed above in your main routine; this should block the SIGTTOU and SIGTTIN signals appropriately on most UNIX systems:You may also need to add #include <signal.h> at the beginning of the file that contains your main routine.Issue the MBUILD command from step 2.

Summary: Memory initialized despite "Remove internal state sero initialization" optimization parameter
Description: Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder does not honor the parameter Remove internal state sero initialization  (settable on the Optimization  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box) when it generates code for a model that requires the RT_MODEL data structure. Embedded Coder disregards the state of the parameter and generates a memset  call in the model _initialize  function, and an associated string.h  inclusion in model .h . Errors will result if the target environment does not support memset  and string.h .
Workaround: To work around this problem in R14sp3, download and install the attached file as follows:Go to the directory matlabroot/rtw/c/tlc/mw/ertlib.tlc (where matlabroot is the MATLAB root directory of your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt) and rename the file ertlib.tlc to ertlib.tlc.old.Download the file ertlib.tlc, listed below under Attachments, and place the file in the same directory as the file you renamed.

Summary: Function-call subsystems: code generated for Assignment block in Function-Call Subsystem block nested in For Interator Subsystem block produces incorrect results
Description: If all of the following conditions exist, Real-Time Workshop generates code that produces incorrect results: The model includes a For Iterator Subsystem block. The for iterator subsystem contains a Function-Call Subsystem block. The function-call subsystem includes an Assignment block.  Under these conditions, local block outputs are initialized for the first function call only. Initialization values for successive function calls are discarded, producing incorrect results. This bug exists in R14SP1 and R13SP3.

Summary: Simulink can fail to initialize a masked subsystem properly
Description: As an optimization, Simulink normally evaluates a masked subsystem's initialization code only once: when loading the model that contains the subsystem. Consequently, if the initialization code causes an error, the masked subsystem can fail to initialize properly.

Summary: Settings of Gauges Blockset blocks change when you copy them from the library
Description: When you copy some preconfigured Gauges Blockset blocks from the library into your model, the block changes some of its ActiveX control settings and resembles another preconfigured block in the same library.  In most cases, a nongeneric block resembles a generic block.
Workaround: Quit MATLAB.Rename the following files by appending .old to the filename:   $MATLAB\toolbox\gauges\ax_block.m   $MATLAB\toolbox\gauges\ax_block.p$MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by entering   matlabrootin the MATLAB Command Window.Download the attached files and place them in the $MATLAB\toolbox\gauges directory.Restart MATLAB.Enter the following in the MATLAB Command Window:   rehash toolboxcacheCopy blocks from the Gauges Blockset library as usual.

Summary: Directly setting RTWStorageClass to Custom for Simulink port objects causes Simulink crash
Description: If you set a Simulink port object's RTWStorageClass  to Custom  directly, without using a Simulink.Signal  object, Simulink crashes during simulation or building.  This is because custom storage classes are only supported via Simulink.Signal  objects. For example, the following commands set Custom  directly and would lead to a Simulink crash:   %% Assume "hPort" is a port handle  op = get_param(hPort, 'Object')  op.RTWStorageClass = 'Custom'
Workaround: You should avoid setting RTWStorageClass to Custom viathe Simulink port object.  To use the Custom storage class, youshould resolve a Simulink.Signal object to a line, and thenspecify Simulink.Signal.RTWInfo.StorageClass to be Custom.

Summary: The C28x QEP block measures rotation speed correctly in only one direction.
Description: When the C28x QEP block is configured to measure rotor speed, its output is correct for only one direction of rotation. If the block is measuring rotation in the other direction, the output might be incorrect.
Workaround: The attached TLC file contains a fix.Place the file in the following directory, relative to your MATLAB root directory:\toolbox\rtw\targets\tic2000\tic2000blks\tlc_c

Summary: Editor/Debugger Save and Run Does Not Stop at Breakpoints
Description: In the Editor/Debugger, when you select Debug > Save and Run  or the equivalent toolbar button, the Editor/Debugger saves the file but does not stop execution at the breakpoints.
Workaround: Because Debug > Save and Run or the equivalent toolbar button saved the file, select the item again (although its name will now be  Run) to execute the file and stop at the first breakpoint.Alternatively, download a patch file to correct the problem as described here: In matlabroot\java, create a directory named patch, where matlabroot is your MATLAB root directory, which you can determine by running the matlabroot function.Quit MATLAB if it is running.Download the attached file, patch.zip, to the patch directory you just added.Unzip the patch.zip file into the same directory. This should create the following directory structure:matlabroot\java\patch\com\mathworks\mde\editorYou should no longer experience the problem.

Summary: The setb option in the power_init command is not available
Description: In the power_init  command, the setb  option allowing setting individual state variables is not available.

Summary: Subsystems: Build Subsystem fails if parent model has underspecified data types
Description: If you use Tools  > Real-Time Workshop  > Build Subsystem  to generate code for a subsystem that resides in a model for which data types are underspecified, Real-Time Workshop might generate a data type propagation error. This can occur even if you are able to update the model diagram successfully. The diagnostic Underspecified data type  on the Diagnostics  > Data Validity  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box,  detects whether a model's data types are underspecified.  However, this diagnostic is set to none  by default.
Workaround: To detect underspecified data types in the model, set the  Underspecified data types  diagnostic to warning or error.

Summary: fi object divide function does not work when a numerictype object is loaded first
Description: The fi  object divide  function does not work when a numerictype  object is loaded first.
Workaround: To work around this problem,  1. Quit MATLAB  2. Restart MATLAB3. Create a fi object before creating a numerictype object.  For example:     a = fi(pi);     T = numerictype;

Summary: Unable to set LineWidth and other edge properties for polygons
Description: Certain problems in the implementation of polygons used in the Map Viewer (mapview ) and in the functions geoshow  and mapshow  make it impossible to set the LineStyle , LineWidth , or Visible  properties for these objects, as well as any marker properties.  In addition, in Version 2.2 of the Mapping Toolbox (R14SP3), applying certain set  commands to a polygon object can cause MATLAB to crash.
Workaround: If you are using Version 2.2 of the Mapping Toolbox (R14SP3), you can apply the following workaround: In your MATLAB root directory, rename the file toolbox/map/map/@MapGraphics/@Polygon/@Polygon.m to toolbox/map/map/@MapGraphics/@Polygon/@Polygon.m-orig.  Likewise, rename the file toolbox/map/map/@MapGraphics/@Polygon/schema.m.  Then copy both of the files in the attachment into toolbox/map/map/@MapGraphics/@Polygon under your MATLAB root directory.

Summary: Axis ticks created with datetick do not adjust automatically.
Description: If you set the x-axis ticks of a plot using the datetick  function, and then zoom in to enlarge the plot, the ticks do not automatically adjust to the new window.  This is expected behavior.
Workaround: Use ticks that are appropriate to the zoomed in state, or call the datetick function again after zooming in.  Specify the 'keeplimits' option when calling datetick.

Summary: Problems with polygon trimming in geoshow when the origin vector is nonzero
Description: Warnings and/or improperly-drawn polygons can result from geoshow  when the map axes has a nonzero origin vector.  Even something as simple as a shift in longitude can cause problems under these conditions.

Summary: Polygons in coast.mat cannot be used with new display capabilities
Description: Mapping Toolbox Version 2.3 (R14SP3) introduced new capabilities for displaying polygons with "holes" (inner rings representing lakes, inland seas, and islands within them), accessible via functions geoshow , mapshow , and mapview . When displaying NaN-separated vectors of latitude-longitude vertices as polygons, these functions assume that outer contours always run clockwise and inner contours run counter-clockwise. In other words, the interior of a feature is always on the right-hand side as one traverses the vertices in order. This convention enables the display functions to fill outer rings properly while leaving inner rings blank. Unfortunately, the new display capabilities cannot be used with the low-resolution coastline sample data file coast.mat  because it originated in an earlier version of the toolbox, in which there was no convention for ordering polygon vertices. (Examples of inner rings in coast.mat  include the great lakes of North America and Africa, the Caspian Sea in Eurasia, and others.)
Workaround: The attachment contains a new version of coast.mat, in which the vertices have been reordered as required to consistently follow the convention used by geoshow, mapshow, and mapview to display polygons with "holes." After backing up the file MATLABROOT/toolbox/map/mapdemos/coast.mat, replace it with the new version.

Summary: Many-to-one data type replacement mapping of char data type causes MISRA-C violation and C++ code generation errors
Description: Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder 4.3 (R14sp3) introduced many-to-one data type replacement, the ability to map multiple built-in data types to one user-defined replacement data type in generated C code.  Attempting to use the built-in data type char  as part of a many-to-one mapping to a user-defined data type introduces a violation of the MISRA-C specification. Specifically, if char  (char_T ) and either int8  (int8_T ) or uint8  (uint8_T ) are mapped to the same user replacement type, the result is a MISRA-C violation. Additionally, if you try to generate C++ code, invalid implicit type casts are made and compile-time errors may result.
Workaround: Use char (char_T) only in one-to-one data type replacements, never for many-to-one data type replacements.

Summary: xPC Target is unable to generate COMiface.dll for models
Description: xPC Target is unable to generate model COMiface.dll , even with the appropriate Configuration Parameters  dialog settings. There is no error message.
Workaround: Quit MATLAB.   Download the attached files and place them in the $MATLAB\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\xpc\ directory. (where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by entering  matlabroot in the MATLAB Command Window.) Restart MATLAB. Enter the following in the MATLAB Command Window:  rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Linked Library Stateflow Charts generate uncompilable code when C++ language is chosen in the RTW configuration tab
Description: Stateflow library charts generate uncompilable code when using the C++ code generation option from RTW configuration settings. The error is due to a missing cast in the generated code. The signature of the compile error isError E2034 .\c1_model.cpp 541: Cannot convert 'void *' to 'SFc1_modelInstanceStruct *' in function sf_c1_model_sim_ctx_io(SimStruct_tag *,const char,FILE *) *** 1 errors in Compile ***
Workaround: To work around this issue, follow these steps:Exit MATLAB.Copy the attached files (code_sfun_glue_code.cdr and code_sfun_glue_code.m) to the directory:<matlabroot>/toolbox/stateflow/coder/privateYou will overwrite the existing files of the same names in this directory.

Summary: System target file: cannot use alternate method for selecting system target file when Configuration Parameters dialog box for model is open
Description: When the Configuration Parameters dialog box for a model is open and you try to change the system target file selection, using one of the following methods, an error message appears indicating that you are trying to recursively change the target. The MATLAB command line The Real-Time Workshop pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog in the Model Explorer for the same model
Workaround: Do one of the following:Change the system target file selection from the Real-Time Workshop pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box that is already open for the model.Close the Configuration Parameters dialog box for your model and then change the system target file selection by using the command line or Model Explorer.

Summary: Context menu from Gauges Blockset ActiveX controls does not work
Description: Right-clicking the active area of a Gauges Blockset block causes the error shown below and the context menu does not appear.   ??? Error using ==> winfun\private\comeventcallback   Error firing event 'MouseUp' to 'ax_block_click'.
Workaround: Quit MATLAB.   Rename the following files by appending .old to the filenames:    $MATLAB\toolbox\gauges\ax_block_click.m   $MATLAB\toolbox\gauges\ax_block_click.p$MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by entering   matlabrootin the MATLAB Command Window. Download the attached files and place them in the $MATLAB\toolbox\gauges directory. Restart MATLAB. Enter the following in the MATLAB Command Window:   rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Incorrect code generation: Incorrect code can be generated with Reuse block outputs enabled
Description: When a model uses a block output signal that is implemented as a local variable, and Reuse block outputs  is enabled, in certain rare cases Real-Time Workshop generates code that overwrites the signal before its final use.  When this happens, the code is incorrect, and its behavior does not match that of Simulink simulation. For example, given a code fragment that Real-Time Workshop should have generated as follows: var_X = expression_X(...)......other expressionsvar_Y = expression_Y(...)......other expressionsvar_Z = expression_Z(var_X,var_Y,othervars)  Real-Time Workshop may incorrectly reuse a variable, resulting in the following code: var_1 = expression_X(...)......other expressionsvar_1 = expression_Y(...)......other expressionsvar_Z= expression_Z(var_1,var_1,othervars)  From the second code fragment, notice how var_1 , which stores the result of expression_X , is reused to store the result of expression_Y ; and how expression_Z , which evaluates var_Z , is using the corrupted var_1  where it actually needs the result from expression_X . Generally, manual inspection of the generated code against the model would lead to detection of the incorrect code. The bug does not occur during normal mode simulation, so (assuming adequate simulation coverage) you can detect the bug's effects by comparing the results of normal mode simulation with the results of the generated code.  For more information on options to make this comparison, please refer to Solution 1-3RX3AI .
Workaround: The MathWorks strongly recommends that you avoid this bug by upgrading to a newer release.  Otherwise, the following workaround is available:In the Configuration Parameters dialog box, select the Optimization pane, then disable the Reuse block outputs option.

Summary: disttool Displays Poisson, Rayleigh, and t Distributions Incorrectly
Description: The disttool  interface displays the incorrect CDF or PDF when you select the Poisson, Rayleigh, or Student's t distributions.
Workaround: In the MATLAB file $matlabroot/toolbox/stats/disttool.m, modify lines 124-125 to read:     'Noncentral Chi-square', 'Normal', 'Poisson', ...    'Rayleigh', 'T', 'Uniform', ...

Summary: CCS built-in plugins are missing when CCS is launched through cc=ccsdsp
Description: When you launch Code Composer Studio with the command ccsdsp , some CCS built-in plugins (you see them in the CCS Tools menu) are missing. This does not happen when you start CCS manually.Software configuration affected CCS version:  CCS 3.0Processor:  C67xThe following table lists the affected boards. Note that the plugins are related to the specific drivers you use.Processor  Driver  Is Plugin Missing    C6711 DSK Blackhawk Emulator Yes   C6711 DSK TI XDS-560 Emulator No  C6711 DSK Parallel port No  C6713 DSK Blackhawk Emulator Yes  C6713 DSK TI XDS-560 Emulator No  C6713 DSK USB Yes

Summary: ActiveX error when passing Simulink model name as parent
Description: If you pass the name of a Simulink model as the parent of an ActiveX control, MATLAB throws an error:??? ActiveX - control creation failed. For example:>>vdp>>h = actxcontrol('mwsamp.mwsampctrl.1',[0 0 100 100],'vdp');
Workaround: Convert the name of the model to a double value by calling get_param. For example:>>vdp;>>parent = get_param('vdp','Handle');>>h = actxcontrol('mwsamp.mwsampctrl.1',[0 0 100 100],parent);

Summary: S-functions: builds fail when name of directory that contains S-function files includes spaces
Description: On Windows systems, if the source files for an S-function are in a directory other than the build directory and the name of the directory includes spaces, builds fail because the make utility cannot locate the source files for compilation.
Workaround: Replace the file rtw_c.m with one of the attached versions. Enter the matlabroot command in the MATLAB Command Window to identify the MATLAB root directory on your system. Quit MATLAB. Rename matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\rtw\rtw_c.p to rtw_c.p.old. Rename matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\rtw\rtw_c.m to rtw_c.m.old. Download the attached file for the release installed on your system, rtw_c_Simulink7p0_fixed.m for R14SP3 or rtw_c_Simulink6p2p1_fixed.m for R14SP2, to matlabroot\toolbox\rtw\rtw\. Rename the downloaded file to rtw_c.m. Restart MATLAB. In the MATLAB Command Window, enter the following commands:rehash toolboxresetrehash toolboxcache

Summary: Updating a model containing an action subsystem can hang or crash Simulink
Description: Updating a model containing an action subsystem can hang or crash Simulink if the action subsystem: contains a subsystem that specifies its sample time and is connected only to blocks that specify their sample times as inherited.  In these circumstances, Simulink hangs if you update the diagram by selecting Edit->Update Diagram  (Ctrl-d ) or crashes if you initiate the update from the command line, i.e., via the model  command.
Workaround: Specify a sample time on the If block or any other blocks connected to the action subsystem.

Summary: Spectrum Scope block yields unexpected plot with sample-based input
Description: The Spectrum Scope block incorrectly interprets sample-based inputs as frame based. This leads to FFTs on scalars for sample-based scalar inputs and FFTs across channels for sample-based vector or matrix inputs.

Summary: ones and zeros cannot generate matrices with more than approximately 400 million elements on 32-bit platforms
Description: On 32-bit platforms (e.g. Windows 2000, Windows XP, MAC, Solaris), there is a limit on the number of elements in an array that can be generated with the functions ones  or zeros . This problem occurs mainly when preallocating large uint8  or int8  arrays.  >> clear>> a=zeros(410e6,1,'uint8');??? Error using ==> zerosMaximum variable size allowed by the program is exceeded. The problem also occurs when indexing results in the expansion of the matrix such as in the following code:  >> clear>> a=uint8(0);>> a(410e6)=0;??? Maximum variable size allowed by the program is exceeded. The limit on the number of elements is approximately 403 million which is smaller than the current matrix size limit in MATLAB which is approximately 1.2 billion elements.

Summary: insert and delete methods fail to set/remove breakpoints when you pass a filename with full path
Description: Setting and removing breakpoints using the syntaxes insert(object,filename,breakpoint) delete(object,filename,breakpoint) fails when you include the full path to the file in filename .These examples demonstrate the problem. Example 1--Insert a breakpoint at line 29 of file foo.c . Define the input arguments as follows.fullfilename = 'c:\temp\foo.c'; % Includes file path.filename = 'foo.c'; % File name only.breakpoint = 29;insert(cc,filename,breakpoint); % This insert breakpoint operation succeeds.insert(cc,fullfilename,breakpoint); % This one fails. insert does not set the breakpoint. Example 2--Delete the breakpoint at line 29 of file foo.c .fullfilename = 'c:\temp\foo.c'; % Includes path to the file.filename = 'foo.c'; % File name only.breakpoint = 29;delete(cc,filename,breakpoint); % delete removes the breakpoint.delete(cc,fullfilename,breakpoint); % Breakpoint is not removed. Note--CCS may crash if you execute a run  operation immediately after an unsuccessful insert breakpoint operation.
Workaround: Make sure you have the correct source file open and active in CCS before you use the insert(cc,filename,linenumber) or delete(cc,filename,linenumber) syntax to insert or remove a breakpoint from filename.This ensures that you are inserting or deleting the breakpoint in the correct file when you pass only the file name to insert or delete, not the full path to the file.For example, start with the following assignments.fullfilename = 'c:\temp\foo.c'; % With path to file.filename = 'foo.c'; % No file path. Name only.breakpoint = 29;Now open and activate the source file.open(cc,fullfilename,'text'); % Opens the source file using the correct path.activate(cc,fullfilename,'text'); % Makes this source file active.Proceed to insert or delete breakpoint(s).insert(cc,filename,breakpoint); % This insert breakpoint operation succeeds.delete(cc,filename,breakpoint); % This delete breakpoint operation succeeds.

Summary: Incorrect Code Generation: When a parallel state exits by a transition from its substate, Stateflow incorrectly executes its siblings
Description: If a parallel state exits by a transition from one of its substates, Stateflow incorrectly executes the during  actions of the sibling parallel states that follow in execution order.
Workaround: Instead of transitioning from a substate, follow these steps:Create a transition directly from the parent OR stateAdd a condition that instructs Stateflow to take the transition only when the substate is active.For example, the attached model fix274996.mdlcontains a Stateflow chart Chart1 that illustrates this workaround. Note that the transition from parent state A contains a condition that uses the in operator to test whether substate C is active, as follows:[in(A.P1.C) && out==1]

Summary: Fixed-point signal logging generates errors for models that contain Stateflow charts
Description: Logging fixed-point signals in models that contain Stateflow charts generates the following error if a Simulink Verification and Validation license is unavailable: ??? Undefined command/function 'cvhtml'.Error in ==> simcoverage\private\generate_results at 147cvhtml(fileName, testObj,reportOptions);Error in ==> simcoverage\private\cv_term_dialog_test at 9generate_results('term', testId);
Workaround: To work around this problem in Simulink 6.3 (R14SP3), download and install the attached patch as described MATLAB the following:Quit MATLAB.Rename the following MATLAB-file:MATLAB\toolbox\simulink\simcoverage\private\cv_autoscaling_settings.mwhere MATLAB is the root directory of your MATLAB installation. Rename the file to, e.g., cv_autoscaling_settings.m.old.Download the attached MATLAB-file found below. Place the new file in the same directory mentioned in Step 2.Restart MATLAB.After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt:rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Adjust Contrast tool displays divide-by-zero warning on double scalar image
Description: For certain double images, imcontrast  is omitting histogram counts. This is one factor that led imcontrast  to produce overly flat histograms.
Workaround: To workaround this bug, download the attached file and put it into the following directory: matlabroot/toolbox/images/imuitools/private

Summary: Globe "projection" uses incorrect formulas for ellipsoidal Earth
Description: The fomulas used in  globe   are correct for the sphere, but the formulas used for the ellipsoid are incorrect. At mid latitudes, globe  places points about 15 km closer to the pole and farther from the equator than they should be.
Workaround: If you are using Version 2.2 of the Mapping Toolbox (R14SP3), you can apply the following workaround:In your MATLAB root directory, rename the file toolbox/map/mapproj/globe.m to toolbox/map/mapproj/globe.m.orig. Then copy the version from the attachment into toolbox/map/mapproj under your MATLAB root directory.

Summary: Uniform Starting Locations in kmeans function
Description: When you call kmeans  and initialize cluster centroid positions using uniform random values, kmeans  generates those starting locations incorrectly. You typically call kmeans  this way when you pass in 'uniform'  as the value of the 'Start'  input parameter.This misinitialization sometimes leads to empty cluster warnings or errors, or to convergence problems.  This problem only occurs if you specify 'uniform'  as the value of the 'Start'  parameter of kmeans .
Workaround: Generate uniform random initial cluster centroid positions before calling kmeans. Then pass those starting locations into kmeans using the 'start' input parameter.  For example, if X is your data matrix, and you want to find k clusters,    Xmins = min(X,[],1);    Xmaxs = max(X,[],1);    Cstart = unifrnd(Xmins(ones(k,1),:), Xmaxs(ones(k,1),:));    [idx,C] = kmeans(X,k,'start',Cstart);

Summary: Models containing blocks whose names begin or end with a forward slash (/) do not load correctly
Description: If a block in a model has a name that begins or ends with a forward slash (/), the model fails to load correctly and produces warnings similar to the following: Warning: Invalid block name '/Gain'.  Block names may not begin or end with /.Warning: untitled.mdl, line 228: Attempt to set DstPort with invalid Destination.Warning: untitled.mdl, line 232: Attempt to set SrcPort with invalid Source. The block connections are lost and the model may produce incorrect results during simulation.
Workaround: Load the model in an older version of Simulink, i.e., Simulink 6.2 (R14SP2) or earlier, and rename all blocks that contain leading or trailing forward slashes to an alternate name.

Summary: Fixed-point addition in Embedded MATLAB can yield an incorrect result when CastBeforeSum = false
Description: Fixed-point addition in Embedded MATLAB can yield an incorrect result when the fimath  property CastBeforeSum  is set to false . For example, consider the addition of two 32-bit integers into an accumulator with fimath  properties CastBeforeSum  = false   RoundMode  = floor   OverflowMode  = saturate    The two inputs being added have the value 231 -1 or 7fffffff in hex, which saturates the 31-bit integer. The accumulator is also an integer, that is, its slope is 1. mymath = fimath(...                'RoundMode','floor',...                'OverflowMode','saturate',...                'ProductMode','KeepLSB',...                'ProductWordLength',32,...                'SumMode','KeepLSB',...                'SumWordLength',31,...                'CastBeforeSum',false);u = fi(2^31-1,1,32,0,'fimath',mymath);y = u+u; The value of y should be 1.0737e+009, or 3fffffff in hex.This can be verified in MATLAB using the Fixed-Point Toolbox.However, in Embedded MATLAB the same code results in -2, or 7ffffffe in hex. Since CastBeforeSum  is false , the addition of the two inputs should be done with full precision, which in the case of our example is 33 bits. The 33-bit result should then get cast into the 31-bit accumulator.  However, because Embedded MATLAB has a word length limit of 32 bits,  the result is computed as a 32-bit integer and then cast into the 31-bit accumulator. This results in precision loss and the incorrect result of -2.
Workaround: The workaround for this problem is to set the fimath property CastBeforeSum to true. This causes both 32-bit inputs to be cast into the 31-bit accumulator type before they are added. This extra quantization results in the expected answer of 1.0737e+009, or 3fffffff in hex.Using the workaround on our example, the code becomes:mymath = fimath(...                'RoundMode','floor',...                'OverflowMode','saturate',...                'ProductMode','KeepLSB',...                'ProductWordLength',32,...                'SumMode','KeepLSB',...                'SumWordLength',31,...                'CastBeforeSum',true);u = fi(2^31-1,1,32,0,'fimath',mymath);y = u+u;The results from the  Fixed-Point Toolbox and Embedded MATLAB are now the same:y =1.0737e+009          DataTypeMode: Fixed-point: binary point scaling                Signed: true            WordLength: 31        FractionLength: 0             RoundMode: floor          OverflowMode: saturate           ProductMode: KeepLSB     ProductWordLength: 32               SumMode: KeepLSB         SumWordLength: 31         CastBeforeSum: true

Summary: Cannot double-click xPC Target Driver demos from the Simulink Library Browser
Description: Release 2.8 introduces the ability to access xPC Target demos from the xPC Target library. If you access the xPC Target library through the Simulink Library Browser and navigate to xPC Target Driver Demos , you cannot double-click on a demo to open it.
Workaround: To work around this issue, you can either:In the Simulink Library Browser left pane, right-click the xPC Target Driver demo you are interested in and select the Open library menu option.In the MATLAB Command Window, type xpclib to open the xPC Target library. Double-click the xPC Target Driver demo you are interested in.

Summary: Function Wizard hangs when executing a function
Description: When you use the Function Wizard with MATLAB Builder for Excel, the application sometimes hangs. This might happen when you use the Function Wizard to execute a function created with MATLAB Builder for Excel in an Excel Worksheet.
Workaround: There are no workarounds for this bug.

Summary: TPU_C TCR1_Clock_Frequency resource configuration property does not update
Description: The value of the TPU_C TCR1_Clock_Frequency property in the MPC555 Resource Configuration block does not update to show the derived value for the TCR1 clock frequency.   The TCR1_Clock_Frequency property is always 0.0.
Workaround: To work around this problem you can replace the MPC555 Resource Configuration block in your model with a new instance of the block from the Embedded Target for Motorola MPC555 Driver Library.

Summary: Some CIC designs do not meet stopband attenuation specification
Description: Some CIC interpolator and decimator designs may not meet the specified stopband attenuation. In some cases, the number of stages is not sufficient to provide the desired attenuation.
Workaround: There is no known workaround.

Summary: All thick line widths look the same in OpenGL with Nvidia graphics cards
Description: When plotting different line thicknesses, thick lines might look too thin or all lines might look the same thickness.
Workaround: There are 2 possible workarounds to this issue.  The first is to set the OpenGLLineSmoothingBug workaround to 1 using the command:opengl('OpenGLLineSmoothingBug', 1);The second workaround is to use software opengl.  You must run the following command before OpenGL has been used the first time in your MATLAB session, so it might be useful to put it in your startup.m file:opengl('software');

Summary: On Macs line objects of zero length are not displayed with Java Figures enabled
Description: If you call line with a single data point, no point is drawn.  For example: l = line(10, 10);   This happens when you run the Sierpinski example.

Summary: The Wind Farm (IG) demo produces an error upon simulation
Description: When you try to run the Wind Farm (IG) demo (power_wind_ig.mdl ) under Distributed Resources Models, it generates the following error:Error using ==> set_param Error evaluating parameter 'Gain' in 'power_wind_ig/STATCOM (Phasor Type)/Power Components Modeling (Shunt & Series Converter)/Bus1/Ki': Error using ==> set_param Undefined function or variable 'Ki'.

Summary: The DC5 One-Quadrant Chopper demo produces errors and warnings upon simulation
Description: The DC5 One-Quadrant Chopper 5 HP DC Drive with Hysteresis Current Control demo (dc5_modified.mdl ) produces errors and warnings upon simulation.

Summary: Simulink block copy fails when simulation mode set to External
Description: If a Simulink model is configured for external mode simulation, copying a block will generate Real-Time Workshop errors.  For example, if you try to copy a block using Ctrl+C  or by selecting Copy  from the Simulink Edit  menu, the model terminates the external mode connection, if one exists, or attempts to compile the model and connect to the target executable. This bug affects any model for which the simulation mode is set to External , including xPC Target and embedded target models.
Workaround: To work around this bug, you can copy and paste one or more blocks using the right-click and drag method in Simulink. Here are the recommended steps:In the model, select one or more blocks to copy.Right-click the blocks and drag them to a new location in the model.Alternatively, you can turn off external mode simulation prior to the copy, copy the blocks, and then reselect external mode simulation.

Summary: Using Ctrl-C in Stateflow Editor puts it into a temporary locked state
Description: Using the Ctrl-C  key to perform the copy operation in the Stateflow Editor causes it to lock up temporarily. During this state, the editor is unresponsive to most actions.
Workaround: For copy operations, use the right-click context menu or select Edit->Copy from the main menu in the Stateflow Editor.

Summary: Activating the main help menu in time series tools creates a command line error
Description: Attempting to open Help from the Time Series Tools Help menu causes the following command line error:??? Error using ==> helpviewSpecified path for file does not exist: ... matlab\help\techdoc\data_analysis\data_analysis.map??? Error while evaluating uimenu Callback. and does not show the help page.
Workaround: Open the current documentation in a Web browser using the following URL:https://www.mathworks.com/access/helpdesk/help/techdoc/data_analysis/f4-6010.html

Summary: Exceed Xterm to Access Editor/Debugger Makes MATLAB Nonresponsive
Description: If you run MATLAB through HummingBird Exceed on a Windows platform and try to open a file in the Editor/Debugger, MATLAB becomes nonresponsive.
Workaround: Using MATLAB in this way is not supported but a workaround for the problem is available.The problem arises because the Editor/Debugger needs to use Exceed XTEST 2.2 for the Robot class, but XTEST 2.2. is not enabled by default. To enable it, in Exceed go to XConfig and to the Protocols section. Then click the Extensions button, and select XTEST(X11R6).

Summary: Workspace Browser Sometimes Shows Error Instead of Values of Variables in Nested Functions
Description: When debugging nested functions, the Workspace Browser sometimes shows this error&lt Error retreiving value &gt instead of the correct values for the variables.

Summary: OPC blockset library demos not accessible from demo browser
Description: The demos in the "Using the OPC Blockset Library" section of the OPC Toolbox demos page show two links at the top of the page. One link says "View code for ..." and the other says "Run this demo."When you click on either of the links, an error is generated in MATLAB. You can safely ignore this error.
Workaround: You can open the demo models in this category by typing the name of the model at the MATLAB command prompt.To open the "OPC Blockset Library Tutorial" demo, typeOPCBlksTutorialTo open the "Example: Distillation Column" demo, typeOPCBlksDistillation

Summary: Periodic sample time constraint errors with variable-step solvers incorrectly reported
Description: If you set the Periodic Sample Time Constraint  option for a fixed-step solver to anything other than unconstrained  and then switch the model to a variable-step solver, Simulink still checks the sample-time constraints even though they should not be. This may result in an error similar to the following: This model will not inherit a sample time because a sample time was explicitly set in block 'vdp/x2'.  or The sample time properties specified on the Solver page are not consistent with the sample times in the model.  The periodic sample time constraints specified are:  [Sample time, Offset time] [1, 0] And the periodic sample times in the model are:  [Sample time, Offset time].
Workaround: Change the solver to a fixed-step solver. This changes the Periodic Sample Time Constraint to Unconstrained. Switch back to the desired variable-step solver.

Summary: lsqcurvefit generates an error when a user-defined output function is passed.
Description: When a user-defined output function is passed to the lsqcurvefit  function via the OutputFcn  option, lsqcurvefit  generates an error message similar to the following:   ??? Error using ==> myoutputfcn  Too many input arguments.  Error in ==> optim/private/snls>callOutputFcn at 537          stop = feval(outputfcn,xOutputfcn,optimValues,state,varargin{:});  Error in ==> optim/private/snls at 264      [xOutputfcn, optimValues, stop] = callOutputFcn(outputfcn,x,xOutputfcn,'init',iter, ...
Workaround: Add the extra argument varargin at the end of the input list of your output function: function stop = myoutputfcn(x,optimValues,state,varargin)This workaround has one particularity in the specific case where you are passing additional parameters P1, P2, ... to your objective function using the old lsqcurvefit syntax shown below -- that is, appending the additional parameters at the end of the input list in lsqcurvefit instead of using nested or anonymous functions: x = lsqcurvefit(FUN,x0,xdata,ydata,lb,ub,options,P1,P2,...)When this old syntax is used, the input cell array varargin will contain the vector xdata in the first element varargin{1) and the additional parameters P1, P2, ... in the remaining elements varargin{2:end}.

Summary: CCS 3.0 issue--resetting CPU prior to DSP execution on C6xxx prevents HS-RTDX from working
Description: This problem occurs only when you run V2.3 (R14SP1+) of Link for CCS with CCS 3.0Resetting your target C6xxx CPU, using eitherDebug->Reset CPU  from the menubar in the CCS IDELink for CCS method reset  before you execute an application prevents HS-RTDX transfers from taking place. After the reset, your C6xxx application waits indefinitely whether it's reading or writing messages.
Workaround: Do not use Debug->Reset CPU in the CCS IDE or the Link for CCS reset method to stop the CPU. Use the Link for CCS halt method instead.

Summary: Simulink Accelerator cannot accelerate models containing blocks named NULL
Description: Simulink Accelerator may not be able to accelerate a model that contains a block named NULL. This is because a compilation error can occur when Simulink Accelerator tries to build a simulation target for such a model. The following is an example of the kind of compilation error that can occur: h:\Documents\untitled_accel_rtw\untitled_acc.h(27) : error C2059: syntax error : 'type'h:\Documents\untitled_accel_rtw\untitled_acc.h(28) : error C2059: syntax error : '}'untitled_acc.c(31) : error C2065: 'untitled_BlockIO' : undeclared identifieruntitled_acc.c(31) : error C2065: 'untitled_B' : undeclared identifieruntitled_acc.c(31) : error C2059: syntax error : ')'untitled_acc.c(32) : error C2275: 'untitled_Parameters' : illegal use of this type as an expression        h:\Documents\untitled_accel_rtw\untitled_acc_types.h(14) : see declaration of 'untitled_Parameters'untitled_acc.c(32) : error C2065: 'untitled_P' : undeclared identifieruntitled_acc.c(38) : error C2059: syntax error : '('
Workaround: Do not name blocks NULL.

Summary: Measurement Computing DAS-1200 D/A is not calibrated
Description: The Measurement Computing DAS-1200 D/A driver analog output levels are incorrect. If you set the output to full scale positive or negative, an opposite full scale reading may result.  This depends on how much the calibration changes the output in hardware.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the attached version of the files dacbpcidas.c and dacbpcidas.dll. Here are the required steps:    Quit MATLAB   Rename the following files:  $MATLAB\toolbox\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks\dacbpcidas.c$MATLAB\toolbox\toolbox\rtw\targets\xpc\target\build\xpcblocks\dacbpcidas.dll  (where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing   matlabroot   at the MATLAB command prompt).  Rename the files todacbpcidas.c.olddacbpcidas.dll.old  Download and unzip the attached file das1200.zip. Place the new files in the same directory mentioned in Step 2.  Restart MATLAB. After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB prompt:  rehash toolboxcache

Summary: dicominfo errors when files do not contain image data
Description: dicominfo fails with the following error message when the specified file does not contain image data, such as radiotherapy (RT) files: ??? Operands to the || and && operators must be convertible to logical scalar values.
Workaround: To work around this issue, replace the version of the dicom_read_mmeta.m file in your current installation with the updated version in the attachment, as follows:Exit MATLAB.Make a backup copy of the following file: matlabroot/toolbox/images/medformats/private/dicom_read_mmeta.mwhere matlabroot represents the name of your MATLAB installation directory, as returned by the matlabroot function. For example, rename the existing file, adding "old" to the file extension: dicom_read_mmeta.m.OLDDownload the attached file, extract dicom_read_mmeta.m, and store it in the directory specified in the previous step.Restart MATLAB.Run the rehash toolbox command at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Certain CAN bit rates cause errors in the Resource Configuration block
Description: Specifying certain CAN bit rates (eg. 83.333k) in the Resource Configuration block causes errors similar to that shown below.   This is because the result of this prescaler calculation has to be a whole number:prescaler = System_Frequency / (Number_Of_Quanta * CAN_Bit_Rate)ERROR : CAN B Error using ==> can_bit_timing System_Frequency / ( Number_Of_Quanta * CAN_Bit_Rate ) must equal a whole number, but, the current settings for these values give:   (20000000 / ( 20 * 83333 ) = 12 You must change one or more of Number_Of_Quanta, CAN_Bit_rate and System_Frequency such that the result is a whole number.
Workaround: The attached version of can_bit_timing.m allows the prescaler result to be within 0.00001 of a whole number, giving an achieved CAN bit rate close to the desired CAN bit rate.You should save a copy of your original file:MATLABROOT/toolbox/rtw/targets/common/tgtcommon/can_bit_timing.mthen replace the original with the attached version.   You may also need to remove or re-pcode the following file:MATLABROOT/toolbox/rtw/targets/common/tgtcommon/can_bit_timing.p

Summary: Integer size other than 8, 16, or 32 can result in the inability to build a Simulink model.
Description: This bug report was nominated and drafted for R14SP3DaCore: If the integer size of a model's embedded target hardware setting is something other than 8, 16, or 32, the model may not be able to complete the Real-Time Workshop build process. Users may see an assertion like this: CGIR assertion in .\base\cg_const.cpp(1045): '0' [0] simulink.dll:void __cdecl assertion_func(void *,char const *,int,char const *)() + 66 bytes [1] cg_ir.dll:void __cdecl client_assertion_failed(char const *,int,char const *)() + 27 bytes
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Enabled subsystems: generated code can exhibit problems in preemptive, multitasking environment
Description: Code generated from enabled subsystems can exhibit nondeterministic behavior and data integrity issues in a preemptive, multitasking environment if both of the following conditions exist: The enabled subsystem uses multiple sample rates. Options on the Solver  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box are set as follows: Type  is set to Fixed-step .  Tasking mode for periodic sample times  is set to Auto  or Multitasking .   The issues that can result are Issue 1:  For multirate enabled subsystems in a multitasking environment, where the rates execute preemptively, nondeterministic behavior can occur due to timing variations associated with enabling and disabling subsystem code. Issue 2:  For enabled subsystems and function-call subsystems that include an asynchronous subsystem, the potential exists for data corruption when data read or write operations are preempted. This can occur when any of the following are true: States when enabling  option for the subsystem's Enable block is set to reset . Output when disabled  option for one or more of the subsystem's Outport blocks is set to reset . A block in the subsystem uses an Enable  or Disable  method that gains access to global data.
Workaround: Two strategies are available for working around this bug. If the first strategy is not feasible, consider the second strategy.Strategy 1:Avoid using multirate enabled subsystems in multitasking environments. An enabled subsystem uses a single rate when all blocks in the subsystem and the enable signal have the same rate.Model changes to consider includeGrouping functionality for each rate into separate enabled subsystems, and converting the enable signal for each subsystem to the same rate as the subsystem by using the Rate Transition block.Avoiding use of Rate Transition blocks inside an enabled subsystem. Consider moving Rate Transition blocks located at the subsystem's internal boundary outside the subsystem such that the subsystem uses a single rate.Using the Rate Transition block to convert input data and enable signals, which drive the subsystem, to the same rate. Also, set all blocks inside the subsystem to that same rate.Strategy 2:Leave the enabled subsystem as multirate, but constrain the sample time of the enable signal by inserting two Rate Transition blocks before the enable port. You should set the Output port sample time option for the first block to the Least Common Multiple (LCM) of all subsystem sample times and the Output port sample time option for the second block to the Greatest Common Divisor (GCD) of all subsystem sample times. For example, if the subsystem sample times are .2 and .3, the LCM is .6 and the GCD is .1.Note that adjusting the sample time of the enable signal in this way may significantly affect the resolution and latency of the enable signal.Detecting the issue:For release R2006a, a Simulink Configuration Parameter diagnostic is provided to detect this issue. Select a value of error for the Sample Time diagnostic titled Multitask conditionally executed subsystem.For previous versions, the utility searchForEnabledSubsystem, which finds multirate enabled subsystems, is attached. Use the utility to locate subsystems that may be affected by this bug.

Summary: ASAP2 interface: problems when generating an ASAP2 interface for models that use data that cannot be addressed
Description: If you use Real-Time Workshop to generate an ASAP2 interface for a model that contains parameters that cannot be addressed in the generated code, the following can result: An error might occur during code generation. The ASAP2 file might contain entries that correspond to data that cannot be addressed.  An example of such a parameter is one that a Custom Storage Class resolves by generating an identifier not associated with memory, such as a macro.

Summary: The File -> Save As dialog in a figure doesn't match the figure file ext.
Description: The figure file extension and the figure file type do not match in the figure's Save As  dialog.  The figure format shows the format of the last saved figure, whereas the extension is always .fig .

Summary: Wind Farm (DFIG detailed model) demo of the Distributed Resources Demos does not work
Description: The Wind Farm (DFIG detailed model demo of the Distributed Resources application library does not simulate because the Initial state vector is not defined. There is a .MAT file associated to the demo that is missing. This file is normally executed at the simulation start in order to define an initial state vector that starts the model in steady state.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the attached versions of the power_wind_dfig_det.mdl and power_wind_dfig_det_xinit.mat files. Here are the steps: Quit MATLABRename the following files: $MATLAB\toolbox\physmod\powersys\DR\DRdemo\power_wind_dfig_det.mdl$MATLAB\toolbox\physmod\powersys\DR\DRdemo\power_wind_dfig_det_xinit.mat(where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typingmatlabrootat the MATLAB Command Prompt.)Rename the files to power_wind_dfig_det.mdl.old and , power_wind_dfig_det_xinit.mat.old respectively.Download the attached files. Place the new files in the same directory mentioned in step 2.Restart MATLAB.

Summary: Block reduction optimization: Real-Time Workshop may fail to generate code when block reduction optimization is enabled
Description: Real-Time Workshop may fail to generate code if the following conditions exist:  The model includes two identical enabled subsystems.  The RTW system code  option on the Block Parameter dialog box for the two subsystems is set to Auto .  Some output signals of one subsystem are connected to Terminator blocks, while the same output signals for the other subsystem are not.  The two subsystems specify different initial condition values.  The Block reduction optimization  option on the Optimization  pane of the Configuration Parameter is selected.
Workaround: Disable the Block reduction optimization option on the Optimization pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box.

Summary: Background textures turn black when using MATLAB interface
Description: Incorrect rendering of virtual scene background textures occur in rare situations using the MATLAB interface of the Virtual Reality Toolbox. The scene is driven using Virtual Reality Toolbox MATLAB interface (not using Simulink VR Sink block) Navigation panel doesn't show in the VR canvas (options 'none' or 'bar') There are only textures used for background definition There is NO definition of background skyAngle and skyColor (or groundAngle/groundColor), or these are empty. In the VRML scene, there is an object with material transparency >= 0.9
Workaround: This issue can be worked around by doing one of the following: For the background, define skyColor/skyAngle fields in addition to textures. This is a good modeling habit anyway, even in the case when all 6 textures are defined for a Background node. Do not hide the navigation panel .When generating an animation recording file, use VRML 3D animation rather than 2D AVI animation.

Summary: Port of Ideal Switch block is visible even if it is turned off
Description: When you turn off the Measurement output port in the Ideal Switch block dialog, the port is still visible in the block icon. It disappears when you update the model or run the simulation.

Summary: The RSDEC function can sometimes fail for values of m greater than 8.
Description: The RSDEC function in the Communications Toolbox allows codewords in the field of characteristic 2^m, where 1<=m<=16.  However, if m is 9 or greater, the RSDEC function can sometimes fail.

Summary: Running a model with unconnected Goto/From blocks causes a segmentation error
Description: Running a model containing unconnected Goto/From blocks with the Unconnected lines  diagnostic set to error  causes a segmentation violation.
Workaround: Set the Unconnected lines diagnostic on the Configuration Parameters->Diagnostics->Connectivity dialog pane to warning or none.

Summary: ellipord may underestimate the order required for bandstop filters
Description: ellipord  may underestimate the order required to meet a given set of specifications for some bandstop filters.
Workaround: It may be necessary to increase the filter order returned by ellipord by one when calling the ellip function in order for the design to meet the specs.

Summary: The Controlled Voltage Source and the Controlled Current Source blocks revert to AC initialization
Description: In a Controlled Voltage Source or a Controlled Current Source block, when you switch the initialization Source type  from AC  to DC  save the block, and close the model this change is ignored. When you open the block again, it reverts to the AC initialization.

Summary: Error running a model containing the Powergui block and no other SimPowerSystems blocks
Description: If you run a configurable subsystem containing SimPowerSystems blocks, then switch to an non-SimPowerSystems block choice and press Run , you get an error message. This error appears when there are no SimPowerSystems blocks in a model except the Powergui block.
Workaround: To work around this problem, delete the automatically created Powergui block after switching to non-SimPowerSystems block choice, but before you click Run.

Summary: grt_malloc target: "Undefined Input Identifier" compilation error during build of model that uses grt_malloc target and Stateflow charts
Description: When all the following conditions exist, an "Undefined Input Identifier" compilation error occurs during a model build:  You are using Stateflow Coder 6.2 (R14SP2).  The model uses Stateflow charts with multiple local event broadcasts or graphical functions.  The model is configured to use the grt_malloc  target.
Workaround: Configure the Stateflow chart to be a reusable function.Right-click the Stateflow chart.Select Subsytem Parameters.Select Treat as atomic unit.Select Reusable function for Real-Time Workshop system code

Summary: Demo power_upfc.mdl does not work
Description: When you try to run the power_upfc demo model, you receive the following error in the dialog box: Index exceeds matrix dimensions.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the attached version of the .MDL and .P-files. Here are the steps:Quit MATLABRename the following file: $MATLAB\toolbox\physmod\powersys\facts\factsdemo\power_upfc.mdl(where $MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typingmatlabrootat the MATLAB Command Prompt.)Rename the file to power_upfc.mdl.oldFollow the same procedure for $MATLAB\toolbox\physmod\powersys\powersys\private\powersolve.pDownload the attached files. Place the new files in the same directory mentioned in step 2.Restart MATLAB.After restarting MATLAB, issue the following command at the MATLAB Command Prompt: rehash toolboxcacheWhen you run the model for the first time, you will receive several warnings that can be safely ignored.

Summary: Using the MATLAB Compiler with Simulink installed can result in a segmentation violation
Description: If Simulink is installed on your system and your MATLAB code contains certain functions, compiling the code with the MATLAB compiler can result in a segmentation violation after the compiler finishes producing an executable or library. The segmentation violation does not affect the compiled output.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Bernoulli Binary Generator block may give incorrect outputs in a multirate model
Description: Using the Bernoulli Binary Generator in a multirate model may give incorrect outputs, if the subsystem blocks are running at different rates. Due to sample-time back-propagation through the relational operator block, the subsystem can run at a rate different from what is specified by a user on the mask.
Workaround: For R14SP3, place the commblkbernoullisrc2.m file in the attached .zip file in the folder <>/toolbox/commblks/commasks/, where <> is the MATLAB installation folder.For R14SP2, place the commblkbernoullisrc2.m file in the attached .zip file in the folder <>/toolbox/commblks/commasks/, where <> is the MATLAB installation folder. Also place the is.m file in the folder <>/toolbox/commblks/commasks/private/.

Summary: Signal Builder signals with forward slashes (/) cannot change index
Description: If you create Signal Builder signals that have forward slashes (/) in their names, you cannot  change the index of the signal. Attempting to change the index corrupts the block and prevents it from working.
Workaround: Temporarily rename the signals to remove any forward slashes before changing the signal index and then rename the signal to its original name.

Summary: Embedded MATLAB Function Blocks take a long time to generate code (appear to hang Simulink)
Description: The Embedded MATLAB Function Block aggressively performs computations at compile time during its analysis.  Blocks containing very large constant computations may take a long time to generate code.For example, the following code appears to hang Simulink:function y = fun(u)  A = ones(200,300);  p1 = reshape(A,300,200);  y = sum(sum(p1))+u;   The expression sum(p1)  is computed when you start the build process. Because the matrices are so large, this computation takes time.
Workaround: The values of Simulink inputs are not known to the Embedded MATLAB Function Block.  To prevent excess compile time computation, make your computation depend on the input values.

Summary: Error when trying to build the TIC6000 model: "(CCS_InstallDir) is not recognized as an internal or external command"
Description: TIC6000 model does not build correctly when full path name for CCS installation directory contains spaces. Building the model (Tools -> Real-Time Workshop ->Build Model ) to generate, download, and run code on the target returns this error:"(CCS_InstallDir) is not recognized as an internal or external command, operable program or batch file". The program does not load and run on the DSP.
Workaround: Make sure that your Code Composer Studio installation directory name (including the complete path) does not have any spaces in it.

Summary: fminsearch passes wrong final function value to output function
Description: The function fminsearch  does not pass the correct final objective function value, stored in optimValues.fval , to the output function. This occurs only when the optimization is finished and the output function is called with its argument state  set to 'done' .

Summary: Connecting a noncontiguous signal to a contiguous input can cause incorrect simulation results
Description: A Simulink model that connects a noncontiguous signal, e.g., a mux or bus signal comprising elements from multiple sources, to a block that requires a contiguous signal may produce incorrect results under the following circumstances: The block requiring contiguous input resides in a triggered, function call, or action subsystem. The model contains blocks operating at different rates. The model is configured to run in multitasking mode.
Workaround: Either insert a Signal Conversion block set to contiguous copy in front of each block that needs contiguous input or configure the model to run in single-tasking mode.

Summary: Axes in FVTool not aligned when viewing more than one response
Description: When FVTool shows two plots in a single display, e.g Magnitude and Phase Response, one set of axes may not be the same size as the other. This causes the resulting axes to be misaligned.
Workaround: To fix this misalignment, make the FVTool figure slightly wider.

Summary: Multirate multitasking models: rate grouping code incorrectly handles projection function
Description: If Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder generates rate grouped code and a projection function for a multirate multitasking model, the following TLC error results: matlabroot /matlab/rtw/c/tlc/lib/utillib.tlc:LibReportFatalError(380) matlabroot/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/sysbodlib.tlc:LibSetSystemField(36) matlabroot /matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/subsystemlib.tlc:FcnSetSystemOpenCloseCodeFieldForTID(3132) matlabroot /matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/subsystemlib.tlc:SLibGenerateSubSystemCode(3506) matlabroot /matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonbodlib.tlc:FcnGenBodyProjectionFcnCache(1050) matlabroot /matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonbodlib.tlc:FcnGenBodySysCache(200) matlabroot /matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/commonbodlib.tlc:SLibGenBodyCache(4023) matlabroot /matlab/rtw/c/tlc/mw/formatwide.tlc(38)
Workaround: If you are using R14SP3, update matlab/rtw/c/tlc/subsystemlib.tlc as follows:Enter the MATLAB command matlabroot to identify your MATLAB root installation directory.Quit MATLAB.Rename the file matlabroot/matlab/rtw/c/tlc/subsystemlib.tlc to subsystemlib.tlc.old.Download the attached version of subsystemlib.tlc to matlabroot/matlab/rtw/c/tlc.Restart MATLAB.Enter the MATLAB command rehash toolboxcache

Summary: Rendering polygons with OpenGL and ZBuffer in geoshow and mapshow
Description: When using the mapshow  or geoshow  commands to overlay one polygon display on another, and using the ZBuffer  or OpenGL  renderer, lines or "streaks" may appear (revealing the underlying tesselation of one of the layers).  For example, the 7th help example for worldmap , does not display properly unless the renderer is set to Painters .
Workaround: Replace Polygon.m and schema.m in toolbox/map/map/@MapGraphics/@Polygon with the versions provided in the workaround for Bug Report  275949. In addition, in order to properly display North America in the 7-th worldmap example, replace toolbox/map/mapdemos/coast.m with the version provided in the workaround for Bug Report  275778.

Summary: fi objects cannot be assigned into uninitialized arrays using subscripting
Description: fi  objects cannot be assigned into uninitialized arrays using subscripting. For example, the following does not work:clearA = fi(1:6);B(1:6,1) = A;??? No method 'isempty' with matching signature found for class 'embedded.fi'.Error in ==> opaque.display at 31if ~isjava(opaque_array) && isempty(opaque_array)
Workaround: To work around this issue, initialize an array to a scalar fi object array before you make any subsequent assignment operations:clearA = fi(1:6);B = A(1);B(1:6,1) = A;

Summary: Pulse Generator cannot compute sample time
Description: The Pulse Generator block, when used in time-based mode with a fixed step solver, sometimes reports the error: No sample time can be computed based on the period, pulse width, and phase delay parameters given. These three parameters, expressed in seconds, must be integer multiples of a fundamental sample time.  This occurs even though the parameters are all integer multiples of a fundamental sample time.
Workaround: Use the Pulse Generator's sample-based mode and specify the desired sample time.

Summary: Documentation missing upgrade note: custom_user_type_registration.m no longer automatically called during code generation
Description: The Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder Release Notes (R14SP2 and R14SP3) are missing an upgrade note relating to a difference in behavior between Embedded Coder 4.1 (R14SP1) and later versions of Embedded Coder. Beginning with Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder 4.2 (R14SP2), the file custom_user_type_registration.m , which you provide if you want to  register user-defined data types, no longer is called automatically during code generation.  Instead, after modifying and saving your custom_user_type_registration.m  file, you must create the Simulink.AliasType  objects corresponding to your user-defined data types before  generating code.  For example, you can: Invoke the MATLAB function ec_create_type_obj  to programmatically create all the required Simulink.AliasType  objects Create Simulink.AliasType  objects one at a time by selecting Add > Simulink.AliasType  in the Model Explorer Create Simulink.AliasType  objects one at a time by entering the MATLAB command userdatatype  = Simulink.AliasType , where userdatatype   is a user-defined data type registered in custom_user_type_registration.m .  For a description of the R14SP2 and R14SP3 procedure for using custom_user_type_registration.m  to register user-defined data types, see "Creating User Data Types" in the R14SP2 or R14SP3 Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder Module Packaging Features document.

Summary: symbol(cc) gives different names to local static variables in CCS 3.0
Description: In CCS 3.0, the command a = symbol(cc) , where cc  is a ccsdsp  object, returns different names for local static variables.CCS appends the string $N  to the symbol name, where N  is an integer the compiler uses to identify the static symbol.For example, if you have the following function prototype in CCS: void foo (void) {   static int array_in[5];   static int array_out[5];   ...}   Here is what you see in MATLAB. cc = ccsdsp;(Now load the program containing the above function.)symbs = symbol(cc)symbs(i).name % Assume 'i' holds the 'array_in' entry.ans =     array_in$1symbs(j).name % Assume 'j' holds the 'array_out' entry.ans =     array_out$2

Summary: iccread returns incorrect value for ConnectionSpace field for certain profiles
Description: The function iccread  returns an incorrect value for the ConnectionSpace  header field for ICC Profiles with a CMYK profile connection space (PCS).  Instead of returning 'CMYK'  for this field, iccread  returns 'RGB' .
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: MATLAB DIMLApp COM interface not registered
Description: The MATLAB DIMLApp COM interface is not correctly registered in the Windows registry. As a result, errors can occur in using the MATLAB COM interface. For example, a program written in C# might contain the following code to refer to a MATLAB COM object: try {      MLApp.MLAppClass matlab = new MLApp.MLAppClass();      matlab.Execute("a = [2 3 4; 3 5 6]");    }catch () {...} Executing this code results in an error message: QueryInterface for interface MLApp.DIMLApp failed.
Workaround: To work around this issue, download the attached file, DIMLApp_regfix.reg. You can run the .reg file when you download it, or you can save the file and double-click it to run it.Caution: Running the .reg file edits the Windows registry. Incorrectly editing the registry can severely damage your system.

Summary: "LoadLibrary failed" error message
