Description: When you deploy a packaged component that you created with MATLAB Builder for Excel to a machine that does not have MATLAB installed, and if you install MCRInstaller with the package, you receive the following error message:LoadLibrary (<Component name>) failed - The specified module couldnot be found
Workaround: As a workaround, register the COM component manually using the regsvr32 utility. To do this enterregsvr32 componentname.dllat the Windows Command Prompt.For instance, if your component is MyComponent, issue the following command:regsvr32 MyComponent.dllVerify that the current working directory is the one that contains the component that you generated with MATLAB Builder for Excel.

Summary: Unable to close Powergui if model is saved under new name
Description: If you have Powergui open when you save a model under a new name using Save As , and then try to close Powergui, you get an error message:Error using ==> get_param Invalid Simulink object name: Old_model_name/powergui.

Summary: Signal Processing Blockset library is no longer pre-built, which can cause build failures
Description: The Signal Processing Blockset library is no longer pre-built and installed as part of the standard Embedded Target for Motorola MPC555 product installation. This can cause build failures if you use blocks from the Signal Processing Blockset in your model. The error message associated with this kind of build failure may look similar to:  D:\matlab\toolbox\rtw\targets\mpc555dk\bin\win32\make.exe: *** No rule to make target 'D:/matlab/toolbox/dspblks/lib/win32/dsp_dyn_rt_5xx_CODE_WARRIOR.a', needed by '../mdsp_ram.s19'. Stop.
Workaround: In release R14SP3, you can build the Signal Processing Blockset library by rebuilding the MPC5xx driver library.  To do this, choose "Rebuild the MPC5xx Driver Library" from the Embedded Target for Motorola MPC555 section of the MATLAB Start Menu. See the product documentation for further details on rebuilding the MPC5xx driver library.

Summary: load and save Do Not Work with Some Java Objects
Description: The load  and save  functions do not work with Java objects you defined on the MATLAB dynamic Java path using javaclasspath  or javaaddpath . The following message appears: Warning: Could not deserialize object
Workaround: Add the Java class to the static classpath (the classpath.txt file) instead of the dynamic Java path. For more information, see javaclasspath documentation.

Summary: For large values of A, B, and M, the MARCUMQ function in the Communications Toolbox can return erroneous answers.
Description: The MARCUMQ function in the Communications Toolbox, invoked by MARCUMQ(A,B,M), computes the generalized Marcum Q function.  If A, B, and M are sufficiently large, the function can return erroneous answers.For example, a range of values of A, B, and M for which MARCUMQ returns potentially erroneous answers is given in the attachment, BadMarcumQVals.txt.
Workaround: There is currently no workaround.

Summary: String property of an edit text component is not updated before a menu Callback is executed.
Description: For an edit text component (a uicontrol  object of style edit ), if you change the string in the edit text component and click on a menu (uimenu  object) before first clicking anywhere else in the figure, The menu's Callback  callback executes before the edit text Callback  does. When the menu Callback  executes, the edit text String  property is not yet updated to reflect the new string.
Workaround: If you want your edit text component's String property to update and its Callback to execute before the menu Callback, then begin the menu Callback with the following lines of code:% Change focus to the figure so that the edit text component loses focus.figure(gcbf);% Force the edit text component to update and execute its Callback immediately.drawnow;  In the menu Callback, you can now access the updated edit text String property with code similar to the following, where handles.edit contains the handle of the edit text.get(handles.edit, 'string')  % Returns the new string.

Summary: Zooming in axes whose aspect ratios have been fixed changes their size
Description: When zooming in on an axes which has had its aspect ratio changed using the command axis equal the axes appear to change size with every zoom operation.

Summary: CCS freezes and hangs when not opened using ccsdsp
Description: CCS version: CCS 3.0  Processors : C6416 DSK, C6713 DSK (no problem with simulators) When you launch CCS manually by double-clicking on a CCS icon or through DOS, and then run the MATLAB code shown below, the CCS window freezes. cc = ccsdsp                % Create ccsdsp object.visible(cc,0)              % Make CCS invisible.open(cc,my_proj_file)      % Open project. % Calling open seems to cause the behavior observed later.load(cc,my_prog_file)      % Load program.ff = createobj(cc,my_func) % Create function object. The outline of the CCS window shows up in the background, but does not become fully visible. The createobj process hangs until you click the CCS window to activate it.
Workaround: There are two ways to avoid this problem. 1) Use ccsdsp to open CCS, and then set the CCS visibility to on.2) If your process requires you to open CCS manually or through DOS, modify your MATLAB code to close the CCS that you opened manually. The following code provides an example of how to do this.cc = ccsdsp       % Create CCSDSP (CCS is already open).visible(cc,0)     % Make CCS invisible.% Add the following three lines of code to your project.clear cc                   % Clears cc, and closes the open CCS.  cc = ccsdsp                % Recreate the ccsdsp object.visible(cc,0)              % Make CCS invisible.% Continue with your project.open(cc,my_proj_file)      % Open project. load(cc,my_prog_file)      % Load program.ff = createobj(cc,my_func) % Create function object.

Summary: Colorbar axis limits on a filled contour plot do not span the full color range
Description: The axis limits on a colorbar attached to a filled contour plot may not span the entire range of colors in the plot.

Summary: Editing English annotations on a Japanese Windows machine can be slow
Description: Editing annotations on a Japanese machine after switching to English mode is really slow.
Workaround: Use the zenkaku Direct input mode to type in English.

Summary: Jiggle mesh parameter setting not saved
Description: If you select Mesh > Jiggle mesh, this setting is not saved when you save your work to an MMATLAB file.

Summary: Compiled Virtual Reality Toolbox application can't find jpeg libraries on HP-UX
Description: An application created using MATLAB Compiler and Virtual Reality Toolbox can't find the required jpeg dynamic libraries.

Summary: Level-2 MATLAB file S-Function blocks: can be optimized away if generated as a constant sample time
Description: A level-2 MATLAB file S-function block can be optimized away from generated code if the block evaluates to a constant sample time. You can prevent this from occuring for a C-Mex S-function by using the option ssSetOptions(S, SS_OPTION_DISALLOW_CONSTANT_SAMPLE_TIME). Level-2 MATLAB file S-functions do not support this option.

Summary: Simulink parameter and signal objects ignore their Min/Max properties
Description: Simulink ignores the Min  and Max  properties of Simulink.Parameter  and Simulink.Signal  objects. As a result, Simulink does not alert you when the object value (specified by the Value  property) falls outside the allowable range set by the Min  and Max  attributes. For example, the following code does not produce any warnings or errors:  a = Simulink.Parameter  a.Min = 10;  a.Max = -10;  a.Value = 52;

Summary: Saving a model to R12 or R12.1 hangs MATLAB
Description: The temporary file created by the Simulink File->Save As  command cannot be closed when using the command to save a model in a form compatible with R12 or R12.1. This renders MATLAB unusable.
Workaround: Quit MATLAB.Locate the following MATLAB file: slsaveassup.m.The file is located at MATLAB\toolbox\simulink\simulink\slsaveassup.mwhere MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine. To find the MATLAB root directory, typematlabrootat the MATLAB command prompt. Rename slsaveassup.m. as slsaveassup.m.oldDownload the attached file.Place the new file in the directory containing slsaveassup.m.old. Restart MATLAB.Issue the following command at the MATLAB command prompt: rehash toolboxcache

Summary: javaclasspath Does Not Show Last Path Added to classpath.txt
Description: When you edit classpath.txt  in matlabroot \toolbox\local , restart MATLAB, and run javaclasspath , the last line in classpath.txt  is not shown. This is only a problem with the display. The missing entry is actually in the Java classpath.

Summary: Triggered subsystems can inherit incorrect sample times
Description: A triggered subsystem can inherit an incorrect sample time if the following conditions apply: The signal driving the subsystem's trigger port comes from a block belonging to a loop of blocks, each of which inherits its sample time. One of the triggered subsystem's inputs runs at a faster rate than that of the trigger signal.
Workaround: Specify the sample time of the block directly connected to the trigger port.

Summary: SimDriveline block name highlighting in the Simulink Diagnostics Viewer
Description: When a model contains two SimDriveline blocks whose names differ only by atrailing digit, the Simulink Diagnostics Viewer incorrectly hyperlinksthe block with the longer name when that block is involved in a simulationerror. Clicking on that block name highlights the incorrect block in the model.

Summary: Clicking a pop-up menu twice disables it on some UNIX window managers
Description: Under some UNIX window managers, clicking the control for a pop-up menu on a Simulink dialog box two times in succession disables the menu.
Workaround: Close and reopen the dialog box.

Summary: sendmail function: messages limited to 7-bit ASCII characters
Description: With the sendmail  function, you can use only 7-bit ASCII characters for messages and subjects.
Workaround: To send characters other than 7-bit ASCII, use an attachment to the message.

Summary: Non-finite values in Sparse Matrix Computations
Description: Since the sparse data structure was introduced in MATLAB 4, sparse algorithms have been optimized to work on the nonzero entries only. This is because operating on zeros results in unstored zeros.  For certain binary operations like times   (.*) and and   (&), when a zero entry interacts with a nonfinite value such as Inf   or NaN  , the result should be a nonfinite value such as NaN  . However, since one operand is zero, the other operand is not even considered and MATLAB returns zero as the result.  This is a known bug that affects the way MATLAB handles sparse matrices with nonfinite values. Note:Binary operations like plus   (+) are not affected by this problem because their algorithms do not make the same assumption as times  . For plus  , if either operand has a nonzero value, then the result also has a nonzero value:    >> A = sparse([0 Inf; NaN 0])   A =      (2,1)      NaN      (1,2)      Inf      >> B = sparse([Inf 0; 0 -Inf])   B =      (1,1)      Inf      (2,2)     -Inf   >> A + B   ans =      (1,1)      Inf      (2,1)      NaN      (1,2)      Inf      (2,2)     -Inf   >> A.*B   ans =      All zero sparse: 2-by-2 The sparse result is different from the corresponding full matrix computation:    >> full(A).*full(B)   ans =      NaN   NaN      NaN   NaN

Summary: Block can inherit incorrect sample time from block loops connected to its input
Description: A block can incorrectly inherit its sample time in the following case: the block's input is connected to at least two loops of blocks that also inherit their sample times. In this case, the block inherits the fastest of the rates inherited by the loops instead of the greatest common divisor of those rates. This can cause incorrect simulation results.
Workaround: Specify the sample time of the block that inherits the incorrect rate or the sample times of the blocks that drive it.

Summary: Help button in the Three-Phase Sequence Analyzer block dialog does not work
Description: When you open the 3-Phase Sequence Analyzer block dialog and click the Help button, the Help window says:  Warning - Documentation File Not Found
Workaround: To access the block reference page, open the Help browser and click SimPowerSystems > Blocks -- Alphabetical List > Three-Phase Sequence Analyzer.

Summary: Cannot drag impixelregion or imoverview rectangles when displaying 16-bit indexed images on PC
Description: If you use the imshow  function to display a uint16 indexed image on a PC, and then activate the impixelregion  or imoverview  tools on the image, you cannot drag the position rectangles these tools overlay on the image.

Summary: Running the LMS Adaptive Filtering demo (rtdxlmsdemo) on a DM642 EVM requires that you load the file DM642EVM.GEL
Description: Running rtdxlmsdemo  on a DM642 EVM requires that you load the file dm642evm.gel . The demo will not run without loading the GEL file because the DM642 EVM loads an incorrect file-- dsp641x.gel --by default. GEL file dsp641x.gel  is not correct to run the demo.
Workaround: Before running rtdxlmsdemo on a DM642 EVM, launch CCS and load the dm642evm.gel file.Launch CCS IDE.Select File->Load GEL from the menu bar. This opens the Load GEL File dialog.Navigate to your CCS installation, browse to find the appropriate GEL file, and select the file to load into your workspace.Click Open to add the GEL file to your workspace and close the dialog.

Summary: Bar plot width arguments ignored with scalar x and y data
Description: When bar(x,y,width)  is called with scalar x  and y  data the width  value is ignored and the default of .8 is used instead.

Summary: Generated code for Multiport Switch block may not be MISRA-compliant
Description: When the Multiport Switch block has more than 2 inputs, its generated code consists of a switch  statement that contains an empty default  section. This code does not conform to MISRA requirements, which do not allow an empty switch  statement default  section.

Summary: Multiple loop operations, or the divide operation can cause segmentation violation
Description: It is possible to obtain a segmentation violation when performing multiple operations of a fi  object within a loop, or when performing the divide  operation of a fi  object.
Workaround: To work around this problem,1. Quit MATLAB2. Rename the following files based on your platform by appending a .old extension. For example, on Windows, rename files to fitools.dll.old and fitools.csf.old:Windows$MATLAB\bin\win32\fitools.dll$MATLAB\bin\win32\fitools.csfLinux 86$MATLAB/bin/glnx86/libmwfitools.so$MATLAB/bin/glnx86/libmwfitools.csfLinux A64$MATLAB/bin/glnxa64/libmwfitools.so$MATLAB/bin/glnxa64/libmwfitools.csfMac$MATLAB/bin/mac/libmwfitools.dylib$MATLAB/bin/mac/libmwfitools.dylib.csfHPUX$MATLAB/bin/hpux/libmwfitools.sl$MATLAB/bin/hpux/libmwfitools.csfSolaris$MATLAB/bin/sol/libmwfitools.so$MATLAB/bin/sol/libmwfitools.csf$MATLAB is the MATLAB root directory on your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt.3. Download the file for your platform from the Attachments section below.Unzip the files and place them in $MATLAB\bin\$PLATFORM, which is the same directory mentioned in Step 2.4. Restart MATLAB

Summary: Disparate behavior of save and load with user-defined objects
Description: If you define a saveobj  method for a superclass, but not for one of its subclasses, MATLAB does not call the superclass saveobj  method when you save an object of the subclass using the MATLAB save  function. Subclasses without saveobj  methods use the default MATLAB save  function.  In constrast, the MATLAB load  function calls the superclass loadobj  method when you load an object of the subclass that does not define its own loadobj  method. While this behavior represents an inconsistency, it is preserved to ensure the backward compatibility of user-written code.
Workaround: You must define a saveobj method for all classes that you create (including all subclasses) if the default behavior of the MATLAB save function is not appropriate for the class.

Summary: Powergui analysis tools do not display dependent state variables
Description: The Steady-State Voltages and Currents  and Initial States Setting  tools in the Powergui block do not display dependent state variables. A dependent state is a state variable that can be expressed as a sum of independent states.

Summary: X-Windows emulator: MATLAB sometimes fails to start
Description: MATLAB sometimes fails to start when you access a UNIX platform using an X-Windows emulator, such as Exceed or Xoftware. An error like the following might appear: Xlib: unexpected async reply (sequence 0x15b)!
Workaround: One workaround is to start MATLAB with the -nodesktop startup option.Another workaround is to remove the MATLABDesktop.xml file from your preferences directory, which is the directory returned by MATLAB when you run the prefdir function. The preferences directory path is similar to this:C:\WINNT\Profiles\...\MATLAB\R14You should then be able to start MATLAB without a problem. Before quitting MATLAB, be sure the desktop is not maximized because that might cause the problem to occur again.

Summary: Clicking a 3-D figure's background in Zoom Mode causes the plot to disappear
Description: When I enable ZOOM-mode and click the background of a figure or the axes tick labels, my plot disappears. After this occurs I am unable to reverse-zoom or return the plot to its original view using the context menu item Reset to original view .
Workaround: A bug exists in MATLAB 7.0 (R14) in how the 'CameraTarget' property of the axes is modified after a zoom event. Even when you click the background of the image, the camera target position still should be within the bounds of the axes, as returned by running the commandget(gcf,'Position')at the MATLAB command prompt. This causes the 'CameraTarget' property to cause the camera to aim at empty space.To work around this issue, select Undo 3-D Zoom from the Edit menu two times.

Summary: Build process: errors can occur when using MATLAB on French Linux systems
Description: If you use MATLAB on a French version of the Linux platform, you can encounter errors such as the following during a Real-Time Workshop build: ??? Failed to parse date string. Error using ==> datevec Failed to lookup month of year.
Workaround: Set the locale (LC_TIME) environment variable as follows:setenv LC_TIME en_USStart MATLAB from the same shell.

Summary: Some Japanese characters in directory name can cause MATLAB Compiler and Builder products to fail
Description: If your code resides in a directory whose name contains certain Japanese characters and you try to compile this code using MATLAB Compiler or  any Builder product, you get compile time errors.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Make errors during code generation for ERT-based Simulink model with non-inlined S-function
Description: Make errors occur during code generation for a Simulink model if the following conditions exist: A Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder target (ert.tlc ) is selected for the model  The model contains a non-inlined S-function  User-defined build arguments are specified using OPTS= , for example, in the Make command  field of the Real-Time Workshop  pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box   The errors occur because Real-Time Workshop Embedded Coder incorrectly overrides the user-defined build arguments.
Workaround: To work around this problem, perform the following steps:Quit MATLAB.Go to the directory matlabroot/toolbox/rtw/rtw (where matlabroot is the MATLAB root directory of your machine, as returned by typing matlabroot at the MATLAB command prompt) and rename the files rtw_c.m and rtw_c.p to rtw_c.m.old and rtw_c.p.old.  Download the files rtw_c.m and rtw_c.p, listed below under Attachments, and place the files in the same directory as the files you renamed.  Restart MATLAB.Refresh the toolbox path cache, for example, by issuing the command rehash toolboxcache at the MATLAB prompt.

Summary: Why does 3-D rotation behave differently in MATLAB 7.0 (R14) than in previous versions?
Description: When I rotate a 3-D figure, the reported elevation angle is very different in the default "Continuous" mode than when I rotate in the "Plot Box" mode. "Plot Box" mode only shows the outline of the new axes view until the mouse button is released when rotating the current axes. "Continuous" mode continuously updates the view of the current axes as the view of the axes is rotated.To observe this behavior, generate a plot by typing:surf(peaks)rotate3d at the MATLAB command prompt. Rotate the axes until the elevation angle is reported as 24 degrees. Next, right-click in the plot and select Rotate Options -> Plot Box Rotate  from the context menu. Now rotate to an elevation of 50 degrees, and then rotate back to an elevation of 24 degrees; the surface clearly looks different.
Workaround: A bug in MATLAB 7.0 (R14) causes axes rotations to be handled inconsistently. Azimuth and elevation together constitute an oversimplification of camera state; camera views involve several parameters, particularly field of view and perspective. This means that replicating a continuous view in Plot Box mode requires experimentation. There are currently no known workarounds.

Summary: Non-double tunable parameter expressions: numeric results produced by generated code may differ from simulation results
Description: In Release 14, MATLAB added support for evaluating expressions that contained variables of types other than double . As a result, Simulink also started supporting evaluation of non-double parameter expressions. Because the rules for non-double math differ between MATLAB and C, the numeric results produced during simulation can differ from results produced by generated C code for tunable  parameter expressions under the following conditions:Rounding of values during conversionSimulation: int8(5.7) ==> int8(6)  C Code: int8(5.7) ==> int8(5)    Saturation of values during conversionSimulation: int8(128) ==> int8(127)  C Code: int8(128) ==> int8(-128)    Downcasting expression results away from doubleSimulation: int8(5) + 2.34 ==> int8(7)  C Code: int8(5) + 2.34 ==> double(7.34)    Saturation of overflow values in integer arithmeticSimulation: int8(100) + int8(28) ==> int8(127)  C Code: int8(100) + int8(28) ==> int8(-128)    Computing chained expressions two at a time with intermediate results returned to homogeneous storage (in contrast to computing them in a larger, 32-bit accumulator that can handle intermediate overflow of smaller data types)Simulation: int8(100) + int8(28) + int8(-10) ==> int8(117)  C Code: int8(100) + int8(28) + int8(-10) ==> int8(118)    Computing integer-integer division in double with the result rounded to the nearest integer (in contrast to the strict C integer division)Simulation: int8(2)/int8(3) ==> int8(1)  C Code: int8(2)/int8(3) ==> int8(0)      Consider the following specific example: K  is a tunable parameter. K = 3.7  Simulation: int8(K) = 4  C code: int8(K) ==> (int8)K ==> 3     This problem concerns tunable parameter expressions only.  For          non-tunable expressions, calculations are done in MATLAB and the results are inlined directly into the generated code. Thus, there are no discrepancies between results.
Workaround: There is no workaround.

Summary: Warning messages associated with the Electric Drives demos and with some other SimPowerSystems blocks
Description: There is a known warning message that is displayed the first time you run the simulation for the demos associated with the Electric Drives library of SimPowerSystems. The warning message informs you that there is some selected signal entering a bus selector block that originates from an unconnected source signal. You can ignore these warning messages. Subsequent simulations of the demos will not produce the warnings anymore.The same erroneous warning message can arise if you use a Switched Reluctance Motor block in a model and you use a Bus Selector to monitor some of the measurement signals of the motor.

Summary: cov now handles empty arrays consistently.
Description: The cov  function handles certain "edge cases" involving empty inputs in a way that is inconsistent with its behavior for non-empty inputs.  cov  also handles certain "edge cases" involving empty vectors in a way that is inconsistent with the behavior of the var  function.  For example,cov([]) cov(zeros(1,0))cov(zeros(0,1))cov(zeros(5,0))cov(zeros(0,5)) all return the scalar 0, while var  would return the scalar NaN for the first three cases.

Summary: The getframe command does not work correctly with docked figures
Description: If you use the getframe  command on a docked figure, the captured image might not be correct.
Workaround: There are two possible workarounds:1. Undock the figure before using getframe.2. Use the print command to export the figure to a standard format image file (e.g., JPEG), and then use the imread command to get the cdata of this file.The following example shows how to use print to export a figure to a JPEG file, 'line.jpg', and how to get the image's cdata from this file using imread.f = figure;line;print(f,'-djpeg','line')a = imread('line','JPEG');

Summary: Plot for task 3 in Debug Example demo (ccsdebugdemo) is incorrect for C27x targets
Description: When you run the Debug Example demo (ccsdebugdemo ) with c27x processor targets, the plot generated in task 3 is not correct.Running task 3 in the demo returns this status message in the CCS output window:Breakpoint Manager: Some breakpoints are not at valid lines. Code Composer has moved these breakpoints to the next valid line. As a result of moving the breakpoint, the demo runs the wrong function and returns the wrong plot.

Summary: Data Ordering in HDF5 Files
Description: hdf5read  and hdf5write  handle data dimensions in an inconsistent fashion.  These functions may also seem inconsistent with respect to other HDF5 data exploration tools. (Note that the ordering of data in HDF5 files is entirely dependent on the application that wrote the HDF5 file. If an application uses row-major ordering, another application may interpret the data in column-major ordering. HDF5 files do not contain any information that indicates how to interpret the dataˇŻs dimensions, e.g., Dim1 = row, Dim2 = column, etc.  Additionally, data exploration applications (such as HDFView and h5dump) can return different results for the same file.)
Workaround: Use the permute command to rearrange the data to use the same data majority as the application that created the file.

Summary: Inaccuracies in cputime on Pentium 4 with Hyperthreading Running Windows
Description: It has been the general rule for CPU-intensive calculations run on Windows machines that the elapsed time using cputime and the elapsed time using tic and toc are close in value, ignoring any first time costs. In the case of a Pentium 4 with hyperthreading running Windows, however, there can be a significant difference between the values returned by these two timing functions. The example shown below compares cputime with tic/toc on two machine architectures: a Pentium 4 with hyperthreading that has two 'logical' processors, and a Pentium M (laptop) with one processor. The case can be best described as a computation that effectively uses both 'logical' processors to the maximum. The example runs two MATLAB scripts called timing and timing2. The scripts call image processing MEX-file functions imadd and imdivide. These MEX-files use a common library called the Intel Performance Primitives Library (IPPL). The imadd function uses routines in IPPL that can effectively use both 'logical' CPUs in a Pentium 4 with hyperthreading 100% while, imdivide uses routines in IPPL that do not use both 'logical' CPUs 100%.  The observed ratio of cputime to tic/toc time is very close to 2, rather than the expected value of 1. This case falsifies the general rule to an extent that, unless additional information is provided (and it is not clear how to get that easily from MATLAB), cputime cannot be recommended to measure performance. We recommend that you use tic/toc in its place. This is an important result because the Pentium 4 with hyperthreading is a fairly common architecture. =====================================================================timing:a = int16(ones(1000)); tic; for i=1:1000, imadd(a,a) ; end; toc;t0 = cputime; for i=1:1000, imadd(a,a) ; end; t = cputime - t0timing2:   a = int16(ones(1000));tic; for i=1:1000, imdivide(a,a) ; end; toc;t0 = cputime; for i=1:1000, imdivide(a,a) ; end; t = cputime - t0===================================================================== Here are the timing results on the two different machine architectures. =====================================================================                      Windows XP                 Windows XP                Pentium 4 (hyperthreading)    Pentium M (laptop)                  timing     timing2          timing   timing2                  ------     -------          ------   -------CPU Utilization:    100%         60%            100%      100%CPUTIME          15.2031      9.9844          7.7500   10.1870TIC-TOC-TIME:     7.6668      9.9848          7.7679   10.2834=====================================================================
Workaround: When using the configuration noted here, we recommend that you use tic/toc in place of cputime.

Summary: FDATool design masks display only if units same as design units
Description: In FDATool, design masks can be displayed only when the magnitude display units are the same as the units used to design the filter.

Summary: Slider uicontrol callback is invoked twice when dragging slider thumb
Description: When you drag the thumb of a slider uicontrol , the uicontrol Callback  is invoked twice. The first invocation is erroneous, and happens at a value close the starting value of the thumb. The second invocation is correct, and happens at the end of the dragging gesture on the slider thumb, as expected.

Summary: Signal label mismatch diagnostic ignores virtual blocks
Description: The signal label mismatch diagnostic (see Configuration Parameters -> Diagnostics -> Connectivity -> Signal label mismatch ) does not check for mismatches between signal labels on output ports of virtual blocks (From, Inport, etc.) driven by the same source.

Summary: UNIX: untar and unzip functions have file permission limitations
Description: On UNIX platforms, the MATLAB untar  and unzip  functions do not preserve file permissions. After extracting the files using untar  or unzip , the user file permissions will always be read and write, while the group and world permissions will be read only.

Summary: ftell After fgets Using fopen 'rt' On Windows Is Incorrect For UNIX Text Files
Description: If fopen 'rt'  mode is used on the PC (Windows) to read a UNIX text file, the output of ftell()  will be incorrect, even when the file is read correctly. This is caused by a bug in the Windows OS.UNIX text files have a linefeed (ascii 10) character at the end of each line. Macintosh text files have a carriage return (ascii 13) at the end of each line. Windows text files have a carriage return (ascii 13) followed by a linefeed (ascii 10) at the end of each line.All 3 types of text files are read correctly on Windows, Linux, and Macintosh using fopen 'r' , 'rb' , and 'rt' . Only the ftell  output is incorrect, and only on Windows when reading a UNIX text file with fopen 'rt'  mode.

Summary: National Instruments PCI-6115 might not output all samples requested
Description: The National Instruments PCI-6115 analog output system might not output all the samples requested.
Workaround: Padding the output data with zeros might be an acceptable workaround.

Summary: Error opening a project that is already open in another CCS IDE window
Description: When a project is already open in a CCS IDE window, using the open  method to open the same project in another CCS IDE window causes the following error in MATLAB:??? Error using ==> ccsdsp (open) OpenProject: The specified project already exists in the collection Note that you can open the same project on two different processors, such as the F2810 and F2812, through the CCS IDE windows.

Summary: Problems accessing some C55x simulator registers in CCS 2.2
Description: When you run the C55x simulator in CCS 2.2, you might encounter problems working with the following registers in the simulator:RSA0RSA1REA0REA1 Using Link for CCS to read from and write to these registers on the simulator generates incorrect results.

Summary: Multiprocessor tutorial (multiproctutorial) times out on OMAP simulator
Description: When you run the tutorial demo multiproctutorial  on the OMAP 3.1 Platform Simulator with OMAP CCS V2.2, the program might generate an error caused by timing issues. Running the tutorial on OMAP hardware does not produce this problem.To reproduce the error through the CCS IDE:Configure your OMAP CCS V2.2 to use the OMAP 3.1 Platform Simulator.Run the tutorial by entering multiproctutorial  at the MATLAB command prompt. A printf  statement in the tutorial program causes this problem on the simulator. In CCS V2.2, the printf  takes a very long time to execute (~45 seconds), causing the process to time out with an error.

Summary: Lcc trigonometric errors affect Simulink Accelerator, Stateflow, and Real-Time Workshop
Description: In C code compiled with Lcc, trigonometric functions with large arguments sometimes return spurious NaNs or other incorrect answers. For example, tanh(20000)  returns NaN. This problem might occur with any function in the C math.h  library. This bug can cause unexpected results with Mex, Simulink Accelerator,  Stateflow simulation, and Real-Time Workshop executables.
Workaround: Avoid large arguments to trigonometric functions in C code compiled with Lcc.

Summary: A sink block may incorrectly precede a function-call initiator in a sorted order
Description: Simulink may incorrectly sort a model's blocks so that a sink block, such as a To Workspace or Scope block, precedes a function-call initiator block, such as a Stateflow Chart block. The problem occurs only if a Merge block drives the sink block and the Merge block appears in the feedback loop of a function-call subsystem. The attached model, m168220.mdl , illustrates the problem.
Workaround: There is no general workaround. If you are migrating from release R14sp3 (or earlier) to release R2006a (or later) you may use the attached M-file script, Detect168220, to determine if the problem existed and was fixed in your model. See the attached MATLAB file script Detect168220 for usage.

Summary: Cannot create objects for C28x register variables
Description: Due to a bug in the development software, you are not allowed to create register objects that access C28x register variables. When you try to create a MATLAB object for a C28x register variable, you receive an error, as in this example.In your CCS source project:register float rl_float = -1.234 At the MATLAB prompt:cc = ccsdsp('boardnum',0,'procnum',0);x = createobj(cc,'rl_float'); results in the error messageCannot create an object for a C28x register variable. This feature is disabled. This limitation occurs because CCS returns the incorrect location for the register variable.

Summary: Goto and From blocks can change propagated sample times
Description: Goto and From blocks can change propagated sample times. When this happens, the sample time of the block connected to the From block differs from the sample time of the From block. This can happen, for example, when a Goto block is connected to the output of an enabled subsystem and Inline Parameters  is off. If Inline Parameters  were on, the block driving the block driving the  subsystem's Outport block would be constant.
Workaround: To workaround this problem you can do any of the following:Remove the Goto and From blocks and directly connect the remaining blocks.Specify the sample time of the block connected to the From block. Add a Signal Specification block between the From block and the blocks connected to it to specify the sample time.

Summary: Build process fails if command line too long
Description: gmake  may fail to build certain models, such as the demo model mpc555rt_ccp.mdl , if the directory name of your MATLAB installation is too long (more than 20 characters).
Workaround: We recommend using an installation directory name of MATLAB7 or equivalent.

Summary: Simulink cannot compute fixed-step size
Description: Simulink is sometimes unable to compute a fixed-step size for a model with a fixed-step size setting of auto . In such cases, Simulink reports an error similar to the following: Fixed-step solver 'FixedStepDiscrete' cannot be used for hybrid block diagram 'techenq5' because a discrete fundamental sample time cannot be determined. Verify that your model contains sample times which are multiples of the fastest sample time specified in your model (i.e. the fixed step size) or use one of the variable-step solvers
Workaround: Set the fixed-step size in the Solver pane of the Configuration Parameters dialog box to a specific value, i.e., a value other than auto.

Summary: Incorrect object created for a C55x 'long long' symbol
Description: When you create an object for a C55x "long long" (40 bits), Link for CCS creates a "long" object  (32 bits) instead. This is also true when you create objects for "unsigned long long" or "signed long long" symbols.The information returned by CCS on a variable having data type "long long" (40 bits) is not correct. The information it returns matches that of a "long" data type which is 32 bits.

Summary: Subsystem connection port warnings
Description: Deselecting Show port  for Body coordinate systems in a Body dialog in a subsystem sometimes leads to port number warnings.

Summary: National Instruments PCI-6110: "Blue screen" crash occurs when using interrupt transfer mode
Description: A Windows kernel crash ("blue screen") can occur at high transfer rates when the TransferMode  property is set to 'Interrupts' . This has been identified as a bug, and we are working with National Instruments to resolve it as soon as possible.
Workaround: As a workaround, set the TransferMode property to either 'SingleDMA' or 'DualDMA'.

Summary: reset(cc) returns control before the board is fully reset
Description: Calling reset(cc)  doesn't always do a true reset due to hardware limitations. Therefore, it may return control before the reset process completes.This poses a problem when your program immediately follows this reset command by another hardware-specific command, such as load(cc,...) . The load  operation fails because the board is not fully reset at that point.
Workaround: To prevent this problem, include a pause after the reset command to give the board time to complete the reset process.Here is an example script that includes the pause.cc = ccsdsp;  % Create CCSDSP handle.reset(cc);    % Reset the board.pause(1);     % Control returns, but gives time for board to complete resetting.load(cc,'myprogram.out'); % safe to perform a hardware-specific operation.

Summary: Camera Toolbar actions "Auto Aspect Ratio" and "Reset Camera and Scene Light" Distort Plot
Description: When using the camera toolbar, selecting the context-menu option "Auto Aspect Ratio" causes the plot to change shape, but that shape can change after further manipulation via the toolbar or otherwise. Using the "Reset Camera and Scene Light" button does not restore the plot to its original configuration, but rather resets it to the canonical 3-D view.
Workaround: No workarounds exist.

Summary: The position associated with VRFIGURE is ignored on UNIX.
Description: When the following commands are executed:    w1 = vrworld('membrane.wrl');  open(w1);  world1 = vrfigure(w1, [200 200 400 400]);    the position data appears to be ignored and the figure opens in the VRSETPREF 'DefaultFigurePosition'  location.
Workaround: The set command can be used to update the vrfigure position.

Summary: C6416 DSK and C6713 DSK ADC and DAC blocks do not error when the input sampling rates are different
Description: If you provide input signals with different sampling rates to the ADC and DAC blocks in a model, no error reports the mismatched sampling rates and code generation proceeds regularly. If the input rate to the DAC block matches one of the valid rates for the audio codec on the target board, no error occurs. Your model might run but generate unexpected results.The DAC block assumes the same sampling rate as the ADC block even though it might be receiving input at a different rate.If you give one sampling rate to the ADC block, say 8000Hz, and feed a signal sampled at a different rate, say 16,000Hz, to the DAC block, code generation should fail.You cannot set the sampling rates of ADC and DAC blocks in C6416 DSK and C6713 DSK targets to different rates. The input rate to the DAC block must be the same as the ADC block.
Workaround: Ensure that the signal fed to the DAC block has the same sampling rate and the same frame size as the ADC block.
